cities.name: Buenos Aires
cities.summary: Buenos Aires is South America's most European-feeling city — grand architecture, world-class steak, and tango on every corner. Petty crime is the main concern, particularly in crowded areas and on public transport. Palermo and Recoleta are safe and vibrant.
cities.quickVerdict: Safe in tourist neighborhoods. Stay in Palermo, Recoleta, or Belgrano. Phone and bag snatching are the biggest risks. Use Uber or Cabify. The peso situation means cash management requires planning.

neighborhoods.name: Palermo
neighborhoods.description: BA's trendiest neighborhood. Palermo Soho and Hollywood have excellent restaurants and bars. Safe to walk day and night on main streets.

neighborhoods.name: Recoleta
neighborhoods.description: Upscale, elegant. Famous cemetery, French-style architecture. Very safe.

neighborhoods.name: San Telmo
neighborhoods.description: Historic, famous for Sunday antiques market and tango. Charming but grittier. Gets sketchier on side streets at night.

neighborhoods.name: La Boca
neighborhoods.description: Caminito is colorful but surrounding area is dangerous. Visit the tourist strip during the day only.

neighborhoods.name: Puerto Madero
neighborhoods.description: Waterfront development. Very safe, well-lit, patrolled. Most secure neighborhood.

neighborhoods.name: Constitución / Once
neighborhoods.description: High crime, bag snatching, muggings even during daytime. Transit through only.

scams.name: Mustard/Bird Poop Scam
scams.description: Someone squirts a substance on you. A 'helpful' stranger cleans you while accomplice picks pockets.
scams.howToAvoid: Walk away immediately if anything is squirted on you.

scams.name: Fake Bills
scams.description: Taxi drivers swap your real bills for counterfeits during change-making.
scams.howToAvoid: Learn to recognize real pesos. Pay with smaller bills.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Long routes, rigged meters. Airport taxis are worst.
scams.howToAvoid: Use Uber or Cabify. For airport, use Tienda León shuttle.

scams.name: Subte Pickpockets
scams.description: Teams work the subway, especially Line D during rush hours.
scams.howToAvoid: Bags in front. No phone on crowded trains.

soloFemale.overview: One of the better South American cities for solo women. Palermo and Recoleta are comfortable. Catcalling exists but is less aggressive. Nightlife starts late and is safe in established venues.
tips: Palermo and Recoleta are most comfortable
BA nightlife starts late — dinner 10pm, bars midnight
Use Uber/Cabify at night
Join tango classes or food tours
San Telmo fine during day, ride-hailing after dark
La Boca — Caminito only

nightSafety.overview: Palermo's bar scene is legendary and safe until early hours. BA has very late-night culture — bars until 4-5am.
tips: Palermo safe until 4-5am
Uber between areas
San Telmo riskier after restaurants close
Phone snatching common at night
Subte stops ~10:30pm

transport.metro: Subte has 6 lines. Cheap, useful during daytime. Crowded rush hour with pickpockets. Buy SUBE card.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Cabify work well. Sit in front.
transport.taxis: Radio Taxis safer than street hails. Check meter.
transport.tips: Uber/Cabify at night. Subte daytime. Tienda León for Ezeiza airport.

customs: Single kiss on right cheek greeting — even between men
Dinner starts 9-10pm
Tipping: 10%, cash preferred
Mate tea is sacred
Football is religion
Argentines love debate

health.overview: Excellent healthcare. Hospital Italiano and Alemán are top-tier.
health.water: Tap water safe but chlorinated.
health.vaccinations: Yellow fever if heading to Iguazú.
health.altitude: Sea level.

faq.q: Is Buenos Aires safe?
faq.a: Yes in tourist neighborhoods. Palermo, Recoleta, Puerto Madero are safe.

faq.q: Safe at night?
faq.a: In Palermo and Recoleta, yes. Use Uber between neighborhoods.

faq.q: Is the subway safe?
faq.a: During daytime with precautions. Stops around 10:30pm.

faq.q: Economic situation?
faq.a: Makes BA extremely affordable for USD/EUR visitors.

faq.q: Is La Boca safe?
faq.a: Caminito strip during daytime only.

cities.name: Medellín
cities.summary: Medellín has transformed from one of the world's most dangerous cities into a thriving hub for tourists and digital nomads. Most visitors have safe, enjoyable trips — but the city still requires awareness. Petty crime, scams, and neighborhood-specific risks are real. Here's everything you need to know.
cities.quickVerdict: Medellín is safe for tourists who stick to well-known areas and exercise common-sense precautions. El Poblado, Laureles, and Envigado are your safest bets. The biggest risks are petty theft, drink spiking (scopolamine), and taxi scams — not violent crime. Don't flash expensive items, use ride-hailing apps instead of street taxis, and never accept drinks or food from strangers.

neighborhoods.name: El Poblado
neighborhoods.description: The main tourist hub. Parque Lleras, Provenza, and the Golden Mile are well-patrolled. Biggest risk is petty theft and drink spiking in nightlife areas. Safe to walk during the day; stick to main streets at night.

neighborhoods.name: Laureles
neighborhoods.description: Slightly more local feel than Poblado. La 70 street has great restaurants and nightlife. Safer at night than Poblado in many ways because there's less tourist-targeting crime.

neighborhoods.name: Envigado
neighborhoods.description: Quiet, residential, and very safe. Feels more authentically Colombian. Great food scene around Parque Envigado. Less nightlife but lower crime rates.

neighborhoods.name: El Centro
neighborhoods.description: Bustling downtown with incredible culture but high pickpocket risk. Visit during the day for Botero Plaza and markets. Avoid after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Comuna 13
neighborhoods.description: Famous for street art and transformation story. Safe for daytime guided tours on the main graffiti routes. Don't wander off the tourist path or visit after 4pm.

neighborhoods.name: Castilla / Aranjuez
neighborhoods.description: Not tourist areas. Higher crime rates, gang presence in some blocks. No reason for tourists to visit. Take an Uber out if you end up here.

scams.name: Scopolamine (Devil's Breath)
scams.description: A drug that renders victims compliant and unable to remember what happened. Administered through spiked drinks, blown powder, laced paper, or perfume samples.
scams.howToAvoid: Never accept drinks from strangers. Don't take flyers or paper from people on the street. Cover your drink at all times. Be cautious with dating app matches who suggest specific bars.

scams.name: Dating App Robbery
scams.description: Attractive profiles on Tinder, Bumble, and Instagram arrange to meet tourists, then spike drinks or lead them to locations where accomplices rob them.
scams.howToAvoid: Meet in public places only. Never go to a stranger's apartment on a first meeting. Tell a friend where you're going.

scams.name: Fake Police Officers
scams.description: Scammers in police-like uniforms stop tourists and demand to inspect wallets, claiming to look for counterfeit bills. They pocket cash during inspection.
scams.howToAvoid: Real Colombian police will never ask to see your wallet on the street. Suggest going to the nearest CAI (police station) together.

scams.name: Overcharging Taxis
scams.description: Some drivers run rigged meters, take long routes, or refuse to use the meter and demand inflated prices.
scams.howToAvoid: Use Uber, InDriver, or DiDi. If using a taxi, confirm the meter is running. Airport to El Poblado should be ~35,000–50,000 COP.

soloFemale.overview: Medellín is visited by many solo female travelers and most have positive experiences. Catcalling is common outside tourist areas. The biggest specific risk is drink spiking — women are disproportionately targeted.
tips: Stay in El Poblado or Laureles for strongest tourist infrastructure and police presence
Use ride-hailing apps exclusively after dark
Share your live location with someone you trust when going out at night
Join a co-working space or hostel common area to meet other travelers
Be cautious with new acquaintances eager to show you around or take you to specific bars
Trust your instincts — if something feels off, leave immediately

nightSafety.overview: El Poblado's Parque Lleras area and La 70 in Laureles are lively and well-lit until 2–3am. Walking main streets in these areas is generally fine. Side streets and alleys can be risky after midnight.
tips: Use Uber or InDriver after midnight, even for short distances
Don't walk alone between neighborhoods at night
Keep your phone in your pocket, not in your hand — phone snatching is common after dark
Avoid displaying jewelry, watches, or expensive clothing in nightlife areas
El Centro (downtown) is not safe after dark

transport.metro: The Metro is clean, efficient, and safe. It's a source of civic pride. The Metrocable system is also safe with incredible views. Watch for pickpockets during rush hour.
transport.rideshare: Uber operates in a legal gray area but is widely used and much safer than street taxis. InDriver and DiDi also popular. Always verify the license plate matches the app.
transport.taxis: Yellow taxis carry higher risk than ride-hailing. Only take taxis from official stands or have your hotel call one. Never hail from the street at night.
transport.tips: The Metro is your best daytime option. Ride-hailing apps are safest overall, especially at night.

customs: Greetings are warm — expect a kiss on the cheek from women and a firm handshake from men
Paisas are famously friendly and proud of their city — showing appreciation goes a long way
Don't refer to cocaine or Pablo Escobar casually — it's disrespectful. Many locals lost family members
Tipping: 10% is standard at restaurants, often automatically included as 'propina'
Paisas tend to dress well — very sloppy appearance draws more attention
Learn basic Spanish phrases — English is not widely spoken outside tourist areas

health.overview: Medellín has excellent healthcare — it's a hub for medical tourism. Hospital Pablo Tobón Uribe and Clínica Las Américas are world-class.
health.water: Tap water is generally safe to drink — one of the few cities in South America where this is the case.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations required for Medellín, but Hepatitis A and Typhoid recommended for Colombia.
health.altitude: Sits at 1,500m — not high enough for altitude sickness, but you may feel slightly more winded for the first day.

faq.q: Is Medellín safe for American tourists?
faq.a: Yes, generally safe with standard precautions. The US State Department rates Colombia at Level 2 — same as the UK, France, and Germany.

faq.q: Is El Poblado safe to walk around?
faq.a: During the day, very safe. At night, stick to well-lit main streets and the busy restaurant/bar areas.

faq.q: Should I avoid Medellín because of its violent past?
faq.a: No. The murder rate has dropped over 95% from its peak. The city has won international urban innovation awards.

faq.q: Is the Metro safe?
faq.a: Yes, one of the safest public transit systems in Latin America. Watch for pickpockets during rush hour.

faq.q: Can I use my phone on the street?
faq.a: Be cautious. Phone snatching is one of the most common crimes. Use discreetly, never leave on restaurant tables near the street.

cities.name: Mexico City
cities.summary: Mexico City is massive, complex, and rewarding. With over 21 million people in the metro area, it's a city of extreme contrasts. Roma, Condesa, and Polanco are as safe as many European neighborhoods. Other colonias carry real risks. The key is knowing where you are at all times.
cities.quickVerdict: Mexico City is safe in the right neighborhoods with standard precautions. Stay in Roma, Condesa, Polanco, or Coyoacán. Use Uber instead of street taxis (this is critical). Don't flash valuables, be aware of your surroundings, and you'll likely have an amazing trip. The food alone is worth it.

neighborhoods.name: Roma / Roma Norte
neighborhoods.description: Trendy, walkable, full of restaurants and galleries. One of the safest areas for tourists. Safe to walk day and night on main streets. The heart of CDMX's food scene.

neighborhoods.name: Condesa
neighborhoods.description: Beautiful tree-lined streets, parks, and café culture. Very safe by day, good at night on main avenues. Popular with expats and digital nomads. Parque México is lovely.

neighborhoods.name: Polanco
neighborhoods.description: Upscale area — Mexico's Beverly Hills. Museums (including the incredible Anthropology Museum), high-end shopping, safe streets. The safest neighborhood for tourists.

neighborhoods.name: Centro Histórico
neighborhoods.description: Amazing architecture and Zócalo, but mixed safety. Main streets are fine during the day with police presence. Gets sketchier at night and on side streets. Don't wander aimlessly.

neighborhoods.name: Coyoacán
neighborhoods.description: Charming village feel, Frida Kahlo Museum. Safe during the day. Central plaza area is fine in the evening. Use Uber to get there and back rather than navigating unfamiliar metro connections.

neighborhoods.name: Tepito
neighborhoods.description: Notorious for crime, counterfeit goods, and drug activity. Extremely dangerous for tourists. Never go here. Even locals exercise extreme caution.

scams.name: Express Kidnapping
scams.description: Victims are forced into a taxi or car and driven to ATMs to withdraw cash under threat. This is real and happens, though less frequently in tourist neighborhoods.
scams.howToAvoid: Never hail taxis from the street — this is the #1 rule in Mexico City. Always use Uber, DiDi, or a sitio (taxi stand) called by your hotel.

scams.name: Fake Uber / Taxi
scams.description: Someone approaches at the airport or busy areas claiming to be your Uber. They're not. Could lead to robbery or overcharging at minimum.
scams.howToAvoid: Always verify the license plate, car model, and driver name match your app before getting in. Never accept rides from people who approach you.

scams.name: ATM Skimming
scams.description: Card skimmers on ATMs, particularly in tourist areas and convenience stores. Also, people watching over your shoulder.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks during business hours. Cover the keypad. Avoid standalone ATMs in convenience stores. Check for loose card readers.

scams.name: Mustard / Ketchup Trick
scams.description: Someone squirts a substance on you, then an 'helpful' stranger offers to clean it off while an accomplice picks your pocket.
scams.howToAvoid: If someone spills something on you, walk away from them before cleaning yourself off. Don't let strangers get close.

soloFemale.overview: Mexico City is visited by many solo women, particularly in the Roma-Condesa corridor. Street harassment (catcalling) exists but is less aggressive than in some other Latin American cities, especially in upscale areas. The metro has women-only cars during rush hour.
tips: Roma, Condesa, and Polanco are comfortable for solo women day and night
The metro has women-only cars (marked in pink) during rush hour — use them
Uber is essential after dark — never take street taxis alone
Be extra careful in Centro Histórico, especially on quieter side streets
The Condesa area has a thriving café and co-working scene where it's easy to meet other travelers
Download the CDMX government safety app for real-time incident reporting in your area

nightSafety.overview: Roma and Condesa have vibrant nightlife and are reasonably safe on main streets. Polanco is safe but quieter. Centro Histórico is best avoided after dark except on well-lit main plazas.
tips: Roma and Condesa main streets are safe for evening walks and dining
Always Uber between areas at night — don't walk between neighborhoods
The Centro Histórico empties out after dark and becomes much less safe
Keep phone use discreet — phone snatching happens
If going to clubs, go with people you know and keep an eye on your drinks

transport.metro: The Metro is cheap and extensive but crowded and pickpocket-heavy. Fine during off-peak hours with basic precautions. Women-only cars available during rush hour. Not recommended late at night.
transport.rideshare: Uber and DiDi are essential in Mexico City. Much safer than street taxis. Always verify the vehicle details. This is the #1 safety tip for CDMX.
transport.taxis: NEVER take street taxis in Mexico City. This cannot be overstated. Express kidnappings via unlicensed taxis are a real risk. Only use app-based rides or sitio (official taxi stand) taxis.
transport.tips: Uber and DiDi are your lifeline. The Metrobús (bus rapid transit) is also safe during daytime. For airport transport, use authorized airport taxis or book an Uber from the official pickup zone.

customs: Greetings involve a kiss on the cheek between women and men-women; handshakes between men
Mexicans are generous and hospitable — if invited to someone's home, bring a small gift
Tipping: 10-15% at restaurants (check if service is included), tip parking attendants R10-20 MXN
Don't be offended if things start late — Mexican time runs on a different clock socially
Food is central to culture — showing genuine enthusiasm for Mexican cuisine is always appreciated
Don't compare Mexico unfavorably to the US — national pride runs deep

health.overview: Mexico City has excellent private hospitals. Hospital Ángeles and ABC Medical Center are top-tier. Private healthcare is affordable by US standards. Travel insurance still recommended.
health.water: Do NOT drink tap water. Use bottled water for drinking and brushing teeth. Most restaurants use purified water and ice, but ask if unsure.
health.vaccinations: Hepatitis A and Typhoid recommended. Routine vaccines should be up to date.
health.altitude: Mexico City sits at 2,240m (7,350ft) — altitude sickness is possible. Take it easy the first day, stay hydrated, limit alcohol initially.

faq.q: Is Mexico City safe for tourists in 2026?
faq.a: Yes, in tourist neighborhoods. Roma, Condesa, Polanco, and Coyoacán are safe and vibrant. The key is knowing which colonias to stick to and using Uber for all transport.

faq.q: Is Uber safe in Mexico City?
faq.a: Yes, Uber is the recommended transport. Much safer than street taxis. Always verify the vehicle matches the app before getting in.

faq.q: Is the Mexico City Metro safe?
faq.a: During daytime and off-peak hours, yes, with basic precautions. Avoid during rush hour if possible (very crowded, pickpockets). Don't use late at night.

faq.q: Is the tap water safe?
faq.a: No. Drink bottled water only. Restaurants use purified water and ice, so drinks and food are usually fine.

faq.q: Will I get altitude sickness?
faq.a: Possibly mild symptoms the first day — headache, shortness of breath. Drink water, take it easy, avoid heavy alcohol on arrival. Most people adjust within 24-48 hours.

cities.name: Bangkok
cities.summary: Bangkok is one of the safest major cities in Southeast Asia for tourists. Violent crime against foreigners is extremely rare. The real risks are scams, petty theft, and traffic accidents. Millions visit safely every year, but the sheer density of tourist-targeting scams means you need to stay sharp.
cities.quickVerdict: Bangkok is safe for most travelers. Violent crime is very low — your biggest concerns are tuk-tuk scams, gem shop cons, overpriced everything, and the occasional pickpocket in crowded areas. Use the BTS/MRT, be skeptical of anyone approaching you near tourist sites, and you'll be fine.

neighborhoods.name: Sukhumvit
neighborhoods.description: Main expat and tourist corridor. Well-lit, tons of restaurants, BTS accessible. Nana and Asoke areas have red-light activity but are safe to walk through.

neighborhoods.name: Silom / Sathorn
neighborhoods.description: Business district by day, nightlife hub by night. Patpong night market area has aggressive touts but low crime. Well-connected by BTS and MRT.

neighborhoods.name: Old City (Rattanakosin)
neighborhoods.description: Where the Grand Palace, Wat Pho, and Khao San Road are. Safe but heavy tourist scam territory. Be skeptical of anyone telling you attractions are 'closed today'.

neighborhoods.name: Khao San Road
neighborhoods.description: Backpacker hub. Safe but chaotic. Drink spiking happens, bags get snatched, and bucket drinks can be dangerously strong. Keep valuables secure.

neighborhoods.name: Chinatown (Yaowarat)
neighborhoods.description: Incredible street food scene. Crowded and chaotic but generally safe. Watch for pickpockets in the dense market areas. Best visited in the evening.

neighborhoods.name: Klong Toei
neighborhoods.description: Contains Bangkok's largest slum. Not a tourist area. Drug activity and occasional violence. No reason to visit unless with a reputable NGO tour.

scams.name: Grand Palace 'Closed Today'
scams.description: Friendly strangers near tourist sites tell you the attraction is closed for a holiday/ceremony and offer to take you to a 'special' temple or shop instead. You end up at a gem shop or tailor with massive markups.
scams.howToAvoid: The Grand Palace is almost never closed unexpectedly. Ignore anyone approaching you outside who claims otherwise. Walk directly to the entrance.

scams.name: Tuk-Tuk Tour Scam
scams.description: Tuk-tuk drivers offer suspiciously cheap rides (10-20 baht) but take you to gem shops, tailors, and travel agencies where they earn commission from your purchases.
scams.howToAvoid: If the price seems too good to be true, it is. Use Grab (ride-hailing app) or metered taxis instead. If you want a tuk-tuk experience, negotiate a fixed price for a specific destination.

scams.name: Gem Shop Scam
scams.description: You're told about a 'government gem sale' or 'once-a-year export opportunity.' The gems are worthless glass sold at massive markups. Often connected to tuk-tuk scams.
scams.howToAvoid: There is no government gem sale. Never buy gems from shops you were taken to. If you want gems, research reputable dealers independently.

scams.name: Jet Ski Damage Scam
scams.description: More common in beach areas like Pattaya and Phuket, but also at river activities in Bangkok. Operators claim you damaged the jet ski and demand thousands of baht.
scams.howToAvoid: Photograph/video the equipment before and after use. Avoid jet ski rentals from operators without clear rental agreements. Have your hotel recommend reputable operators.

soloFemale.overview: Bangkok is one of the safer Asian cities for solo female travelers. Thai culture is generally respectful, and violent crime against women tourists is rare. The main risks are the same scams that affect everyone, plus some unwanted attention in nightlife areas.
tips: Bangkok is very welcoming to solo female travelers — many women travel here alone without issues
Dress modestly when visiting temples (cover shoulders and knees) — this is cultural respect, not just safety
Be cautious in Khao San Road area late at night — drink spiking does occur
Use Grab (Southeast Asia's Uber) rather than hailing random taxis, especially at night
Full moon party areas and Patpong can feel aggressive — you can avoid them entirely without missing much
Thai people are generally helpful — if you feel uncomfortable, duck into any 7-Eleven (they're everywhere) and ask staff for help

nightSafety.overview: Bangkok is remarkably safe at night compared to most major cities. Sukhumvit, Silom, and the Old City stay busy well past midnight. Street food vendors and 7-Elevens provide constant foot traffic and lit areas.
tips: Main tourist areas are safe to walk at night — Bangkok never really sleeps
Use Grab for late-night transport rather than hailing taxis off the street
Khao San Road gets rowdy after midnight — keep valuables secured
Avoid dark sois (side streets) in unfamiliar areas
Drink spiking happens — watch your drink, especially in tourist nightlife areas

transport.metro: BTS (Skytrain) and MRT (subway) are excellent — clean, safe, air-conditioned, and cheap. Best way to get around. Can get very crowded during rush hour.
transport.rideshare: Grab is the main ride-hailing app. Reliable, metered, and safer than street taxis. Bolt also operates. Always use the app rather than negotiating with drivers directly.
transport.taxis: Metered taxis are cheap but some drivers refuse to use the meter or take long routes. Always insist on the meter. If they refuse, get another taxi.
transport.tips: BTS/MRT for anything on the route. Grab for everything else. Avoid tuk-tuks unless you want the experience and are willing to pay tourist prices. River boats (Chao Phraya Express) are great for riverside attractions.

customs: Never disrespect the Thai monarchy — lèse-majesté laws are strictly enforced with prison sentences
Remove shoes before entering temples and homes
Don't touch anyone's head — it's considered the most sacred part of the body
Don't point your feet at people or Buddha images — feet are considered the lowest part of the body
The wai (palms together bow) is the standard greeting — return it when offered
Tipping is not traditionally expected but appreciated. 20-50 baht for good service, round up taxi fares

health.overview: Bangkok has excellent private hospitals — Bumrungrad International is world-renowned. Public hospitals are adequate but crowded.
health.water: Do NOT drink tap water. Bottled water is cheap and available everywhere. Ice in restaurants is usually from purified water and is safe.
health.vaccinations: Hepatitis A and Typhoid recommended. Consider Japanese Encephalitis for longer stays or rural travel.
health.altitude: No altitude concerns. Heat and humidity are the main health factors — stay hydrated and use sunscreen.

faq.q: Is Bangkok safe for first-time travelers?
faq.a: Yes, excellent for first-timers. Well-developed tourist infrastructure, friendly locals, cheap and safe public transit. Start in Sukhumvit area for easiest orientation.

faq.q: Is Bangkok safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, one of the safer major Asian cities for solo women. Thai culture is respectful, violent crime against tourists is rare. Standard precautions around nightlife apply.

faq.q: Are tuk-tuks safe in Bangkok?
faq.a: Physically safe, but financially risky. Most tuk-tuk 'deals' are scams to take you to commission shops. Use Grab or metered taxis for actual transport.

faq.q: Is street food safe to eat?
faq.a: Generally yes — Bangkok's street food is legendary and usually safe. Look for stalls with high turnover and freshly cooked food. Avoid anything sitting out for long periods.

faq.q: Do I need to worry about political protests?
faq.a: Bangkok has periodic political demonstrations. Avoid protest areas if they occur. Check local news and your embassy's advisories. Protests rarely affect tourist areas directly.

cities.name: Cairo
cities.summary: Cairo is 7,000 years of history, the Pyramids, the Nile, and 22 million people in constant motion. Violent crime against tourists is very rare. The overwhelming challenge is the non-stop hustle — touts, scams, and aggressive selling at every tourist site. Women face significant harassment. But the history is incomparable.
cities.quickVerdict: Safe from violent crime. The Pyramids, Egyptian Museum, and Khan el-Khalili are incredible. Touts and scams are relentless. Women will face constant harassment. Hire guides, use Uber, and embrace the chaos.

neighborhoods.name: Zamalek
neighborhoods.description: Island neighborhood. Embassies, galleries, upscale restaurants. Safest area for tourists. Feels calmer.

neighborhoods.name: Garden City
neighborhoods.description: Near Tahrir Square. Government buildings. Relatively safe and central.

neighborhoods.name: Maadi
neighborhoods.description: Expat suburb. Tree-lined streets, restaurants. Safe and comfortable.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown / Tahrir
neighborhoods.description: Chaotic central area. Egyptian Museum. Fine during day. Busy and loud.

neighborhoods.name: Islamic Cairo / Khan el-Khalili
neighborhoods.description: Historic district. Amazing mosques and bazaar. Tout central. Safe daytime.

neighborhoods.name: Giza Plateau
neighborhoods.description: The Pyramids. Safe with guide. Aggressive touts, camel scams. Incredible experience.

scams.name: Pyramid Camel Scam
scams.description: Free camel ride to see the pyramids. Then they won't let you off until you pay hundreds of dollars.
scams.howToAvoid: Never accept free rides. Negotiate firmly before mounting. Better: book a proper guide.

scams.name: 'My Father's Papyrus Shop'
scams.description: Friendly locals direct you to shops where you're pressured to buy overpriced goods.
scams.howToAvoid: Don't follow strangers. Choose your own shops.

scams.name: Fake Closed Attractions
scams.description: Touts claim your destination is 'closed for prayers' and redirect you.
scams.howToAvoid: Ignore. Walk directly to the entrance. Same as Bangkok's Grand Palace scam.

scams.name: Perfume Shop Hustle
scams.description: Invited in for 'just tea' then aggressive perfume/oil sales.
scams.howToAvoid: Decline invitation. If you enter, you'll be there an hour.

soloFemale.overview: Cairo is one of the more challenging cities for solo female travelers. Sexual harassment — verbal and physical — is a serious and well-documented issue. It's doable with preparation but requires constant vigilance.
tips: Zamalek and Maadi are most comfortable
Dress very modestly — loose clothing covering arms and legs
Harassment is not your fault — it's a documented societal issue
Uber essential — don't use public transport alone
Hire a male guide for pyramids and Islamic Cairo
Consider group tours — much easier and safer
A fake wedding ring may slightly reduce attention

nightSafety.overview: Cairo is a late-night city — dinner at 9-10pm is normal. Zamalek has restaurants and Nile-side cafés. Khan el-Khalili is atmospheric. Uber after dark.
tips: Zamalek safe for evening dining
Khan el-Khalili atmospheric but Uber in/out
Nile dinner cruises are safe and enjoyable
Downtown gets quieter and less safe
Uber after dark always
Shisha cafés are great evening activity

transport.metro: Cairo Metro has 3 lines. Cheap but extremely crowded. Women-only cars available and recommended.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Careem work well. Essential.
transport.taxis: White taxis — insist on meter or agree price. Black taxis are older, less reliable.
transport.tips: Uber for most transport. Metro possible during off-peak with caution.

customs: Egypt is conservative — modest dress shows respect
Remove shoes entering mosques
Tipping (baksheesh) is expected everywhere — small change constantly
Ramadan affects everything — eat/drink discreetly in public
Don't photograph military or government buildings
Egyptian Arabic greetings appreciated — 'ahlan' (hello)

health.overview: As-Salam International Hospital. Cairo has good private hospitals.
health.water: Do NOT drink tap water. Ice is usually fine in tourist restaurants.
health.vaccinations: Hep A, Typhoid. Polio booster if not current.
health.altitude: 75m — no issues. Extreme heat in summer. Air quality poor.

faq.q: Is Cairo safe?
faq.a: From violent crime, yes. Scams and harassment are the challenges.

faq.q: Safe for solo women?
faq.a: Challenging. Harassment is constant. Doable with preparation.

faq.q: How to see the Pyramids?
faq.a: Hire a licensed guide. Book in advance. Don't accept 'free' rides.

faq.q: Is the Egyptian Museum worth it?
faq.a: The new Grand Egyptian Museum near the Pyramids is world-class.

faq.q: Tap water safe?
faq.a: No. Bottled only.

cities.name: Cape Town
cities.summary: Cape Town is one of the most beautiful cities on Earth — and one that requires genuine caution. Tourist areas like the V&A Waterfront and Camps Bay are safe, but Cape Town has extreme inequality and the crime that comes with it. The city rewards informed visitors with unforgettable experiences, but going in naive can lead to serious problems.
cities.quickVerdict: Cape Town is safe in tourist areas with proper precautions, but requires more awareness than most Western cities. Stick to known safe areas, don't walk alone at night outside tourist zones, use Uber exclusively, and don't display valuables. The beauty is absolutely worth the extra vigilance.

neighborhoods.name: V&A Waterfront
neighborhoods.description: The safest area in Cape Town. Shopping, restaurants, private security. Safe to walk around day and night within the complex. Essentially a self-contained safe zone.

neighborhoods.name: Camps Bay
neighborhoods.description: Affluent beachside suburb. Very safe, beautiful sunsets, good restaurants. Safe to walk during the day. Don't leave valuables visible in parked cars.

neighborhoods.name: City Bowl / Gardens
neighborhoods.description: Central Cape Town. Safe during business hours with people around. Gets quieter and riskier at night. Kloof Street area has good nightlife and is relatively safe.

neighborhoods.name: Sea Point
neighborhoods.description: Popular promenade for walking and jogging. Safe during the day, okay early evening. Don't walk the promenade alone after dark. Good restaurants along the strip.

neighborhoods.name: Woodstock
neighborhoods.description: Gentrifying arts district. Great during the day for markets and galleries. Borders rougher areas — don't wander too far from the main streets. Uber in and out.

neighborhoods.name: Cape Flats
neighborhoods.description: Extremely dangerous. High gang activity, shootings, and violent crime. Absolutely no reason for tourists to go here. This includes Khayelitsha, Nyanga, and Mitchell's Plain unless on a reputable guided township tour.

scams.name: Car Break-ins
scams.description: Anything visible in your car will be stolen — guaranteed. Smash-and-grab at traffic lights also happens. Thieves watch parking lots and strike quickly.
scams.howToAvoid: Never leave anything visible in your car. Use the trunk before arriving at your destination (not while being watched). Keep windows up and doors locked at traffic lights.

scams.name: Fake Parking Attendants
scams.description: Unofficial car guards demand payment for 'watching' your car. Some are legitimate informal workers, others are not. Refusing can occasionally lead to your car being vandalized.
scams.howToAvoid: Give R5-R10 to avoid trouble — it's not worth the conflict. Use secure parking garages when possible. Some areas have legitimate city-appointed car guards with vests.

scams.name: Mugging at Scenic Spots
scams.description: Isolated scenic viewpoints and hiking trails (Signal Hill, Lion's Head, Table Mountain paths) are targeted by muggers, especially early morning and late afternoon.
scams.howToAvoid: Hike in groups, never alone. Start popular hikes when others are around. Don't carry expensive cameras/phones visibly. Stick to well-trafficked paths.

scams.name: Beach Theft
scams.description: Belongings left on the beach while swimming are stolen quickly. Organized thieves watch for distracted tourists.
scams.howToAvoid: Never leave bags unattended on the beach. Use a waterproof pouch for phone and cards. Take turns watching belongings.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Cape Town requires extra precautions compared to European cities. The tourist areas are fine, but South Africa has high rates of gender-based violence. Staying aware and choosing safe areas makes a huge difference.
tips: Stay in Camps Bay, Waterfront, or Sea Point for the safest base
Always use Uber — never walk alone at night outside the Waterfront or Camps Bay strip
Join group tours for hiking and township visits
Don't walk between neighborhoods — Uber even for short distances
The Waterfront is the safest area for evening dining and entertainment
Consider staying at hostels or hotels with good social scenes to meet other travelers for group activities

nightSafety.overview: Cape Town's night safety is limited to specific areas. The Waterfront, Camps Bay restaurants, and Kloof Street in the City Bowl are fine. Beyond these zones, risk increases dramatically after dark.
tips: V&A Waterfront is safe to walk around at night — private security is everywhere
Camps Bay main strip is safe for evening dining
Kloof Street / Long Street area has nightlife but use Uber door-to-door
Do not walk between areas at night — even short distances
Keep phone use discreet — phone snatching is common

transport.metro: MyCiTi bus system covers tourist routes and is reasonably safe during daytime. The train system (Metrorail) is NOT safe for tourists — avoid it entirely. Muggings on trains are common.
transport.rideshare: Uber is your best friend in Cape Town. Reliable, safe, and reasonably priced. Use it for everything, especially at night. Bolt also operates here.
transport.taxis: Metered taxis exist but are more expensive than Uber and less reliable. Don't hail from the street. Your hotel or restaurant can call a reputable company.
transport.tips: Uber for everything. Seriously. Don't rent a car unless you're doing the Garden Route or wine country day trips, and even then, lock everything in the trunk.

customs: South Africans are warm and friendly — a simple 'howzit' (hello) goes a long way
Cape Town is very diverse — English is widely spoken along with Afrikaans and Xhosa
Tipping: 10-15% at restaurants, R5-10 for car guards, R20-50 for tour guides
Load shedding (power outages) is a reality — check the schedule and keep devices charged
Don't discuss apartheid politics with strong opinions unless you really understand the nuances
Braai (barbecue) is a cultural institution — if invited to one, it's a genuine honor

health.overview: Cape Town has excellent private healthcare. Netcare Christiaan Barnard Memorial Hospital is world-class. Private healthcare is expensive — travel insurance is essential.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink in Cape Town — some of the best municipal water in Africa.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations required for Cape Town. Yellow fever certificate needed if arriving from an endemic country. Malaria is not a risk in Cape Town.
health.altitude: No altitude concerns. Sun is extremely strong — use high SPF sunscreen. The UV index regularly hits extreme levels.

faq.q: Is Cape Town safe for tourists?
faq.a: In tourist areas, yes — with precautions. The Waterfront, Camps Bay, and Constantia are safe. But Cape Town has stark inequality and high crime rates overall. Stay informed about which areas to avoid.

faq.q: Is Uber safe in Cape Town?
faq.a: Yes, Uber is the recommended transport method. Drivers are vetted, trips are tracked, and it's much safer than public transport or walking.

faq.q: Can I walk around Cape Town?
faq.a: During the day in tourist areas, yes. But distances between safe areas can cross unsafe ones. Use Uber between neighborhoods rather than walking.

faq.q: Is Table Mountain safe to hike?
faq.a: Safe in groups on popular routes during busy times. Don't hike alone, start early, stick to main paths, and don't carry expensive visible gear.

faq.q: Should I visit a township?
faq.a: Only with a reputable tour operator. Never go independently. Guided township tours are educational and generally safe with established companies.

cities.name: Johannesburg
cities.summary: Johannesburg has a high crime rate — this is not exaggeration. But Sandton, Rosebank, and Melrose are safe, modern, and vibrant. The city rewards brave visitors with incredible history (Apartheid Museum, Soweto), art, and food. Just follow the rules strictly.
cities.quickVerdict: Sandton is safe and modern — Africa's richest square mile. Outside safe zones, crime is serious. Uber everywhere. Never walk between areas. Don't drive after dark in unfamiliar areas. Soweto with a guide only.

neighborhoods.name: Sandton
neighborhoods.description: Africa's financial hub. Modern malls, restaurants, hotels. Private security. Very safe.

neighborhoods.name: Rosebank
neighborhoods.description: Trendy area. Galleries, market, restaurants. Safe during day, good at night around the mall.

neighborhoods.name: Melrose / Melville
neighborhoods.description: Artsy neighborhoods with restaurants. Fine during day. Use Uber at night.

neighborhoods.name: Maboneng
neighborhoods.description: Regenerating inner city. Great on market days. Declining safety otherwise. Don't walk from CBD.

neighborhoods.name: CBD / Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Inner city. Dangerous for tourists. Muggings, carjackings. Do not walk. Uber through only.

neighborhoods.name: Alexandra / Soweto
neighborhoods.description: Townships. Soweto is fascinating but guided tour only. Alexandra is dangerous.

scams.name: Smash and Grab
scams.description: Car windows smashed at traffic lights to grab phones, bags, valuables.
scams.howToAvoid: Windows up, doors locked. Nothing visible. Be alert at intersections.

scams.name: Carjacking
scams.description: Armed carjacking at gates, driveways, traffic lights.
scams.howToAvoid: Check mirrors at gates. Don't stop for strangers. Drive to police station if followed.

scams.name: ATM Shoulder Surfing
scams.description: Teams watch PIN, then distract while card is swapped.
scams.howToAvoid: ATMs inside malls only. Cover PIN. Cancel if approached.

scams.name: Fake Police
scams.description: Fake roadblocks or officers demand wallet.
scams.howToAvoid: Drive to nearest police station. Real officers have visible station numbers.

soloFemale.overview: Not recommended for solo female travelers without local knowledge. If visiting, stay in Sandton, use Uber exclusively, and join organized tours. South Africa's GBV rates are among world's highest.
tips: Stay in Sandton or Rosebank
Uber for literally everything
Never walk between areas
Join organized tours for Apartheid Museum, Soweto
Mall culture is huge — safe dining/shopping
Consider combining with Cape Town for better solo experience

nightSafety.overview: Sandton has restaurants and bars that are safe. Don't drive unfamiliar areas after dark. Uber door-to-door.
tips: Sandton restaurants safe
Nelson Mandela Square dining area good
Uber door-to-door always
Don't drive unfamiliar routes at dark
Melville 7th Street has bars — Uber in/out
CBD absolutely not at night

transport.metro: Gautrain is safe and modern — Sandton to OR Tambo airport. Don't use Metrorail.
transport.rideshare: Uber essential. Only safe transport option for most situations.
transport.taxis: Not safe from the street. Uber only.
transport.tips: Uber for everything. Gautrain for airport. Do not use minibus taxis.

customs: South Africans are warm — 'howzit' is universal greeting
11 official languages — English is lingua franca
Tipping: 10-15% at restaurants, R5-10 car guards
Apartheid history is raw and recent — listen more than opine
Load shedding means carry a power bank
Braai (BBQ) is sacred culture

health.overview: Netcare and Mediclinic hospitals excellent. Private healthcare world-class.
health.water: Tap water safe in Joburg.
health.vaccinations: None required for Joburg specifically. Yellow fever if from endemic country.
health.altitude: 1,753m — mild altitude. Dry climate. Thunderstorms in summer (Oct-Mar).

faq.q: Is Joburg safe?
faq.a: Sandton and Rosebank are safe. Crime is serious elsewhere. Follow the rules strictly.

faq.q: Should I visit?
faq.a: If interested in South African history and culture, yes. Apartheid Museum is powerful.

faq.q: Can I walk around?
faq.a: In Sandton, between malls and nearby restaurants. Otherwise Uber.

faq.q: Is Soweto safe?
faq.a: With a reputable guide, yes. Don't go independently.

faq.q: Uber safe?
faq.a: Yes and essential.

cities.name: Marrakech
cities.summary: Marrakech is sensory overload — the medina, the souks, Jemaa el-Fnaa. It's safe from violent crime but the hustle is relentless. Scams, aggressive touts, and disorientation in the medina are the main challenges. Once you adjust, it's unforgettable.
cities.quickVerdict: Safe from violent crime. The medina is overwhelming but fascinating. Scams and aggressive salespeople are the biggest annoyance. Hire a local guide for day one. Learn to say 'la shukran' (no thank you) with conviction.

neighborhoods.name: Medina (tourist areas)
neighborhoods.description: Ancient walled city. Beautiful riads, souks. Overwhelming. Safe on main routes but easy to get lost.

neighborhoods.name: Jemaa el-Fnaa
neighborhoods.description: Famous main square. Entertainers, food stalls, chaos. Pickpockets active. Amazing atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: Gueliz
neighborhoods.description: New town. French colonial feel. Restaurants, cafés, galleries. Much calmer than medina.

neighborhoods.name: Hivernage
neighborhoods.description: Upscale hotel district. Safe, modern, quiet.

neighborhoods.name: Kasbah area
neighborhoods.description: Near Saadian Tombs and El Badi Palace. Tourist area. Fine during day.

neighborhoods.name: Outskirts / Palmeraie
neighborhoods.description: Palmeraie has luxury hotels but isolation. Don't walk between properties.

scams.name: Medina 'Guides'
scams.description: Young men offer to lead you through the medina. They demand payment and may lead you to shops for commission.
scams.howToAvoid: Hire an official guide through your riad. Use GPS. If lost, enter a shop and ask.

scams.name: Souk Price Inflation
scams.description: First price can be 10x the real value. Vendors are skilled negotiators who read your interest.
scams.howToAvoid: Bargain to 25-40% of first price. Walk away — they'll call you back. Know approximate prices.

scams.name: Jemaa el-Fnaa Snake Charmers
scams.description: Snake charmers and monkey handlers place animals on you then demand 200+ MAD for photos.
scams.howToAvoid: Don't engage. Walk away immediately if anything is placed on you. A photo should be 10-20 MAD.

scams.name: Henna Artists
scams.description: Women grab your hand and start applying henna before you agree. Then demand 300+ MAD.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep hands away. Firm 'la shukran.' If you want henna, negotiate price first.

soloFemale.overview: Morocco is challenging for solo female travelers — catcalling and persistent male attention are constant outside tourist hotels. Many women visit successfully but it requires thick skin. Traveling with a companion or joining group tours reduces hassle significantly.
tips: Dress modestly — cover shoulders and knees in medina
Catcalling is persistent — ignore completely, don't engage
Riads are safe and welcoming
Hire a guide for first medina exploration
Gueliz is much calmer than medina
Consider a female guide — several excellent ones operate

nightSafety.overview: Jemaa el-Fnaa comes alive at night with food stalls and entertainers — it's safe in the crowded areas. Gueliz has modern bars. Walking in the medina at night is fine on main routes but avoid dark alleys.
tips: Jemaa el-Fnaa safe in crowds
Main medina routes lit and walked
Avoid empty alleys at night
Gueliz has modern nightlife
Riad staff can arrange taxi
Medina shuts down by 11pm mostly

transport.metro: No metro. The medina is walking only (cars can't enter).
transport.rideshare: Uber doesn't operate. Careem works in some areas. Petit taxis most common.
transport.taxis: Petit taxis (beige) for within city. Insist on meter. If refused, agree price first. Medina to Gueliz: 20-30 MAD.
transport.tips: Walk in medina. Petit taxi to Gueliz/Hivernage. CTM bus for intercity.

customs: Morocco is Muslim — respect prayer times and Ramadan
Remove shoes at mosques (non-Muslims can't enter most)
Left hand is unclean — eat, give, receive with right hand
Tipping: 10% at restaurants, 10-20 MAD for guides
Bargaining is expected and enjoyed in souks
French and Darija Arabic — learn a few phrases of each

health.overview: Clinique Internationale in Gueliz. Limited compared to European standards.
health.water: Do NOT drink tap water.
health.vaccinations: Hep A, Typhoid recommended.
health.altitude: 450m — no issues. Extremely hot in summer (40°C+).

faq.q: Is Marrakech safe?
faq.a: Safe from violent crime. Scams and aggressive touts are the challenge.

faq.q: Safe for solo women?
faq.a: Challenging but many do it. Catcalling persistent. Dress modestly, ignore harassment.

faq.q: How bad are the scams?
faq.a: Constant in the medina. Once you learn to say no firmly, it becomes manageable.

faq.q: Is Jemaa el-Fnaa safe at night?
faq.a: Yes, the crowded food stall area is safe. Avoid empty surrounding streets.

faq.q: Should I bargain?
faq.a: Absolutely. Start at 25-30% of asking price. Walking away is the most powerful tool.

cities.name: Nairobi
cities.summary: Nairobi is East Africa's vibrant hub — tech scene, incredible food, Nairobi National Park with wild lions visible against the skyline. But crime is a genuine daily reality. Carjacking, phone snatching, and mugging are common. Safe areas are very safe; unsafe areas are very unsafe.
cities.quickVerdict: Westlands, Karen, and Gigiri are safe and vibrant. Don't walk between areas. Uber or Bolt everywhere. Phone snatching constant. The National Park, Giraffe Centre, and Elephant Orphanage are wonderful and safe.

neighborhoods.name: Westlands
neighborhoods.description: Modern hub. Sarit Centre, restaurants, nightlife. Safe during day, good at night in commercial areas.

neighborhoods.name: Karen
neighborhoods.description: Upscale suburb. Giraffe Centre, Karen Blixen Museum. Leafy and safe.

neighborhoods.name: Gigiri
neighborhoods.description: UN compound area. Very safe, diplomatic zone. Village Market mall.

neighborhoods.name: Kilimani / Hurlingham
neighborhoods.description: Residential expat areas. Reasonably safe. Don't walk at night.

neighborhoods.name: CBD / Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Busy during day but phone snatching and mugging rampant. Don't walk with valuables.

neighborhoods.name: Eastlands / Eastleigh
neighborhoods.description: High crime. Do not visit. Kibera guided only.

scams.name: Phone Snatching
scams.description: Phones grabbed on the street constantly. The most common crime against visitors.
scams.howToAvoid: Never use phone on the street. Keep in deep pocket.

scams.name: Matatu Pickpockets
scams.description: Minibus taxis (matatus) are pickpocket havens.
scams.howToAvoid: Use Uber or Bolt instead.

scams.name: Carjacking
scams.description: Armed carjacking, especially at slow traffic or gates.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep windows up, doors locked. Don't stop for strangers. Use Uber.

scams.name: Fake Safari Deals
scams.description: Non-existent or terrible safari packages sold cheaply.
scams.howToAvoid: Book through established operators. Read TripAdvisor reviews.

soloFemale.overview: Challenging for solo women. Kenya is warm and welcoming but Nairobi has serious crime. Stay in Westlands or Karen, use Uber, and join organized safaris.
tips: Westlands or Karen safest bases
Uber or Bolt for all transport
Don't walk with phone visible — ever
Organized safari from Nairobi is the main draw
Java House chain is safe meeting spot everywhere
Nairobi National Park is incredible and safe

nightSafety.overview: Westlands has good nightlife. Don't walk — Uber door-to-door. Karen is quiet at night. CBD dangerous after dark.
tips: Westlands clubs and restaurants are fun
Uber door-to-door — no exceptions
CBD not safe after dark
Karen quiet evenings
Don't carry valuables going out
Kenyan nightlife is vibrant

transport.metro: No metro. New Nairobi Expressway helps traffic.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Bolt work well. Essential.
transport.taxis: Only use Uber/Bolt or hotel-arranged.
transport.tips: Uber/Bolt for everything. Don't use matatus. SGR train to Mombasa is modern and safe.

customs: Kenyans are warm and hospitable
English widely spoken along with Swahili
Tipping: 10% at restaurants, tip safari guides generously
Don't photograph people without asking
Safari etiquette: quiet, stay in vehicle
Nairobi means 'cold water' in Maasai — bring layers for evenings

health.overview: Nairobi Hospital and Aga Khan University Hospital excellent.
health.water: Do NOT drink tap water.
health.vaccinations: Yellow fever, Hep A, Typhoid. Malaria prophylaxis if going on safari.
health.altitude: 1,795m — mild altitude. Cool evenings.

faq.q: Is Nairobi safe?
faq.a: In Westlands, Karen, Gigiri — manageable. Serious crime elsewhere.

faq.q: Safari from Nairobi?
faq.a: Yes — Masai Mara, Amboseli, Nairobi National Park all accessible.

faq.q: Uber safe?
faq.a: Yes and essential.

faq.q: Phone snatching?
faq.a: Very common. Never use phone on the street.

faq.q: Worth visiting beyond safari?
faq.a: Yes — food scene, tech hub, culture. But safari is the main draw.

cities.name: Athens
cities.summary: Athens is ancient history come alive — the Acropolis, Plaka, and the Agora are breathtaking. The city is generally safe. Pickpockets on the metro and around tourist sites are the main concern. Anarchist graffiti is everywhere but rarely affects tourists.
cities.quickVerdict: Safe with standard European precautions. Plaka and Kolonaki are charming and safe. Pickpockets active on metro and tourist sites. The Acropolis is unmissable. Greek food and hospitality are wonderful.

neighborhoods.name: Plaka
neighborhoods.description: Ancient neighborhood below Acropolis. Tavernas, shops, atmosphere. Tourist-friendly and safe.

neighborhoods.name: Kolonaki
neighborhoods.description: Upscale. Boutiques, cafés, Lycabettus Hill. Safe and elegant.

neighborhoods.name: Monastiraki
neighborhoods.description: Flea market, great restaurants. Crowded — pickpocket territory.

neighborhoods.name: Psyrri
neighborhoods.description: Nightlife and street art. Gentrifying. Fine during day, some sketchy corners at night.

neighborhoods.name: Exarchia
neighborhoods.description: Anarchist neighborhood. Graffiti, alternative culture. Some drug activity. Interesting but polarizing.

neighborhoods.name: Omonia
neighborhoods.description: Central square. Pickpockets, drug activity. Transit through only. Improving slowly.

scams.name: Metro Pickpockets
scams.description: Professional teams on metro, especially Line 3 to airport and at Syntagma.
scams.howToAvoid: Bags in front. Hand on wallet. Be alert when doors open.

scams.name: Restaurant Bill Padding
scams.description: Extras added to bill — bread basket charged, wrong items.
scams.howToAvoid: Check prices on menu. Review bill carefully.

scams.name: Fake Leather/Fur
scams.description: 'Greek leather' goods that are synthetic.
scams.howToAvoid: Buy from established shops in Plaka.

scams.name: Uber Equivalents
scams.description: Taxi drivers hostile to ride-hailing.
scams.howToAvoid: Beat app works. Regular taxis also fine.

soloFemale.overview: Athens is good for solo women. Greece is safe, people are friendly, and the café culture is perfect for solo travelers. Standard precautions.
tips: Plaka and Kolonaki very comfortable
Solo dining very normal — Greek taverna culture
Metro safe but watch bags
Acropolis at opening to avoid crowds
Island ferries leave from Piraeus — easy connection
Greek coffee and conversation culture perfect for solo travelers

nightSafety.overview: Psyrri and Gazi have nightlife. Plaka pleasant evenings. Monastiraki square lively. Athens has a late dining culture.
tips: Psyrri and Gazi bar districts
Plaka safe for evening dining
Monastiraki square atmospheric
Omonia less safe at night
Metro until midnight
Greek nightlife starts late

transport.metro: Three metro lines. Clean, safe. Pickpockets on Line 3.
transport.rideshare: Beat (formerly Taxibeat) is the main app. Uber limited.
transport.taxis: Yellow taxis metered. Generally honest.
transport.tips: Metro excellent. Beat app for taxis. Walking best in center.

customs: Greeks eat dinner late — 9-10pm
Tipping: round up or 5-10%
Don't mistake Greek head gestures — lifting chin means 'no'
Respect ancient sites — don't climb on ruins
Coffee culture is serious — don't rush
Greek Easter is bigger than Christmas

health.overview: Good private hospitals. EU EHIC accepted.
health.water: Tap water safe in Athens.
health.vaccinations: None required.
health.altitude: 70m. Hot dry summers. Wildfires possible.

faq.q: Safe?
faq.a: Yes, standard European safety. Pickpockets main concern.

faq.q: Acropolis tips?
faq.a: Go at opening. Book tickets online. Bring water and hat.

faq.q: Is Exarchia safe?
faq.a: Interesting but polarizing. Fine during day. More edge at night.

faq.q: Greek islands from Athens?
faq.a: Ferries from Piraeus. Book at ferries.gr. Fly for distant islands.

faq.q: Is the food good?
faq.a: Incredible. Avoid tourist traps near Acropolis entrance. Walk 2 blocks for real food.

cities.name: Barcelona
cities.summary: Barcelona is one of Europe's most visited cities — and its pickpocket capital. Violent crime is very rare, but petty theft is an epidemic. La Rambla, Metro, and beaches are prime hunting grounds. Once you secure your valuables, the city is extraordinary.
cities.quickVerdict: Very safe from violent crime. Pickpockets are the #1 issue and they're professionals. Zip everything, use money belts, and you'll be fine. The architecture, food, and beaches are incredible.

neighborhoods.name: Eixample
neighborhoods.description: Cerdà grid. Sagrada Família, Passeig de Gràcia. Safe and beautiful.

neighborhoods.name: Gothic Quarter
neighborhoods.description: Medieval streets. Atmospheric but prime pickpocket territory.

neighborhoods.name: El Born
neighborhoods.description: Trendy. Picasso Museum, bars, restaurants. Safe and charming.

neighborhoods.name: Gràcia
neighborhoods.description: Local village feel. Cafés, plazas. Very safe. Park Güell nearby.

neighborhoods.name: La Rambla
neighborhoods.description: Famous boulevard. Tourist trap and pickpocket capital. Walk it once, eat elsewhere.

neighborhoods.name: Raval
neighborhoods.description: Multicultural. Some great restaurants. Rougher at night. Southern Raval less safe.

scams.name: Pickpocketing
scams.description: Professional teams on La Rambla, Metro, beaches, Sagrada Família. Distraction techniques — petitions, flower giving, group crowding.
scams.howToAvoid: Money belt. Bags zipped and in front. Nothing in back pockets. Be alert in crowds.

scams.name: Beach Theft
scams.description: Leave bags while swimming — gone in seconds.
scams.howToAvoid: Take turns. Use waterproof pouch. Don't bring valuables.

scams.name: Fake Petitions
scams.description: Clipboard petition is distraction while accomplice picks pockets.
scams.howToAvoid: Ignore. Don't stop. Keep walking.

scams.name: Street Games
scams.description: Shell games on La Rambla — you cannot win. Accomplices in crowd.
scams.howToAvoid: Don't watch. If you play, you will lose money.

soloFemale.overview: Excellent for solo women. Spain is one of the safest European countries. Barcelona's beach and food culture is perfect for solo travel. Standard nightlife precautions apply.
tips: Very safe for solo women
Eixample and Gràcia most comfortable
Solo dining totally normal
Metro safe during day — watch bags
Beach safe but watch belongings
Nightlife starts late — dinner 9pm, bars midnight

nightSafety.overview: El Born and Eixample have excellent nightlife. Barcelona stays out late. La Rambla at night is pickpocket territory. Raval less safe after midnight.
tips: El Born and Eixample safe at night
Barcelona nightlife starts very late
Metro runs until midnight (Fri/Sat later)
La Rambla pickpockets active at night too
Raval south end less safe late
Beach area fine for early evening

transport.metro: Excellent metro system. Watch for pickpockets — especially L3 and L4.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Cabify work.
transport.taxis: Yellow and black, metered, safe. Airport to center ~€35-40.
transport.tips: Metro is excellent but guard bags. T-Casual card for 10 rides.

customs: Dinner starts 9-10pm
Tipping: round up or 5-10% max
Catalan pride — learn 'gràcies' (thank you in Catalan)
Siesta culture — many shops close 2-4pm
Don't go to La Rambla restaurants — tourist traps
Book Sagrada Família tickets months in advance

health.overview: Hospital Clínic and Hospital del Mar good. EU EHIC card accepted.
health.water: Tap water safe but tastes chlorinated. Most drink bottled.
health.vaccinations: None required.
health.altitude: Sea level. Mediterranean climate.

faq.q: Is Barcelona safe?
faq.a: From violent crime, very safe. Pickpockets are professional and pervasive.

faq.q: How bad are pickpockets?
faq.a: Worst in Europe. Professional teams. Take it seriously.

faq.q: Is La Rambla worth it?
faq.a: Walk it once. Don't eat or shop there.

faq.q: Metro safe?
faq.a: Yes, but guard belongings on L3 and L4 especially.

faq.q: Solo female travel?
faq.a: Excellent. Spain is very safe for women.

cities.name: Istanbul
cities.summary: Istanbul is remarkably safe for a city of 16 million. Violent crime against tourists is very rare, and the city is well-policed in tourist areas. The main risks are pickpockets in crowded areas, carpet/restaurant scams, and the ever-present earthquake risk. Most visitors love it and feel safe throughout their stay.
cities.quickVerdict: Istanbul is safe for tourists who use common sense. Sultanahmet and Beyoğlu are well-patrolled. Your main risks are pickpockets at the Grand Bazaar, friendly strangers leading you to overpriced restaurants, and taxi drivers taking scenic routes. The city has excellent public transit and a welcoming culture.

neighborhoods.name: Sultanahmet
neighborhoods.description: Historic heart — Blue Mosque, Hagia Sophia, Topkapi Palace. Very safe, very touristy. High pickpocket risk at the Grand Bazaar. Tourist police patrol regularly.

neighborhoods.name: Beyoğlu / Taksim
neighborhoods.description: İstiklal Avenue is lively and safe. Great restaurants and nightlife around Karaköy and Galata. Can get crowded and chaotic, watch for pickpockets.

neighborhoods.name: Kadiköy (Asian Side)
neighborhoods.description: Trendy, local, and very safe. Great food scene, less touristy. Easy ferry ride from European side. One of the safest areas in the city.

neighborhoods.name: Beşiktaş
neighborhoods.description: Lively university area. Local feel with good restaurants and bars. Safe for walking day and night. Good base for exploring the Bosphorus.

neighborhoods.name: Fatih (beyond Sultanahmet)
neighborhoods.description: More conservative area. Generally safe but less tourist infrastructure. Women may receive more attention. Fine during the day, less comfortable at night.

neighborhoods.name: Tarlabaşı
neighborhoods.description: Just off İstiklal Avenue but a different world. Drug activity, muggings, and generally unsafe for tourists. Easy to accidentally wander in — turn around if you see the area looking run-down.

scams.name: Friendly Shoe Shiner
scams.description: A shoe shiner 'accidentally' drops his brush near you. When you pick it up or call out to him, he insists on shining your shoes out of gratitude, then demands an outrageous fee.
scams.howToAvoid: Don't pick up the brush. If someone starts shining your shoes uninvited, walk away immediately. Say 'hayır' (no) firmly.

scams.name: The Friendly Local Bar
scams.description: A friendly Turkish man strikes up conversation, suggests going to a nice bar. Attractive women join. At the end, you get a bill for hundreds or thousands of dollars. Bouncers ensure you pay.
scams.howToAvoid: Never go to a bar or restaurant suggested by a stranger who approaches you on the street. Choose your own venues. If a situation feels like a setup, leave before ordering.

scams.name: Carpet Shop Hustle
scams.description: You're invited for 'just tea' in a carpet shop, then subjected to hours of high-pressure sales tactics. The carpets are real but massively overpriced.
scams.howToAvoid: It's fine to accept tea (Turkish hospitality is genuine), but don't feel obligated to buy anything. 'I'll think about it' and leaving is perfectly acceptable.

scams.name: Taxi Meter Tricks
scams.description: Drivers set the meter to the night rate during the day, take long routes, or claim the meter is broken and demand a flat fee.
scams.howToAvoid: Use BiTaksi app (Turkey's ride-hailing). If using a street taxi, confirm 'gündüz' (daytime rate) is set. Know the approximate fare — airport to Sultanahmet is 250-350 TL.

soloFemale.overview: Istanbul is visited by many solo women and is generally safe. Turkish culture values hospitality, and locals are often genuinely helpful. However, catcalling and persistent male attention are more common than in Western Europe, particularly outside main tourist areas.
tips: Tourist areas (Sultanahmet, Beyoğlu, Kadiköy) are comfortable for solo women day and night
Dress slightly more conservatively in the Fatih area and when visiting mosques — this reduces unwanted attention
Men may be persistent in conversation — a firm but polite 'no thank you' usually works
Use BiTaksi app rather than hailing taxis on the street at night
The Asian side (Kadiköy, Moda) tends to feel more relaxed and modern
Turkish women are very helpful if you need assistance — don't hesitate to approach them

nightSafety.overview: Istanbul is generally safe at night in tourist and entertainment areas. İstiklal Avenue, Karaköy, and Kadıköy stay busy until late. The city has an active nightlife culture and well-lit main streets.
tips: Beyoğlu and Karaköy are safe and lively at night
Avoid Tarlabaşı and back streets off İstiklal after dark
The Asian side shuts down earlier but feels very safe
Use BiTaksi app for transport after midnight
Be cautious of the 'friendly local bar' scam — it primarily operates at night

transport.metro: Istanbul's metro, tram, and ferry system is extensive and safe. Get an Istanbulkart (transit card) — it works on all public transport. The tram through Sultanahmet is excellent for tourists.
transport.rideshare: BiTaksi is the main ride-hailing app. Uber was banned but may return. BiTaksi shows you the fare upfront and is much safer than street taxis.
transport.taxis: Yellow taxis are everywhere but scams are common. Always insist on the meter and verify the rate setting. Airport transfers are better booked through BiTaksi or your hotel.
transport.tips: The ferry between European and Asian sides is one of the best transit experiences in the world — safe, cheap, and beautiful views.

customs: Remove shoes when entering mosques — head coverings are provided for women at major mosques
Turkish hospitality is legendary — offering tea is a social custom, not always a sales tactic
Bargaining is expected at the Grand Bazaar and markets, but not in regular shops or restaurants
Tipping: round up taxi fares, 5-10% at restaurants if service isn't included
Don't blow your nose loudly in public — it's considered rude
Political discussions (especially about Erdogan or Kurdish issues) are best avoided with strangers

health.overview: Istanbul has good private hospitals. Acibadem and Memorial are well-regarded. EU health insurance (EHIC) does not cover Turkey — travel insurance is important.
health.water: Tap water is technically safe but tastes heavily chlorinated. Most locals drink bottled or filtered water. Bottled water is very cheap.
health.vaccinations: No specific vaccinations required. Routine vaccines should be up to date. Hepatitis A recommended for longer stays.
health.altitude: No altitude concerns. Earthquake risk is real — Turkey sits on major fault lines. Know your hotel's evacuation plan.

faq.q: Is Istanbul safe for American tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Istanbul is safe for Americans. The US State Department rates Turkey at Level 2. Tourist areas are well-policed and millions of Americans visit without incident.

faq.q: Is Istanbul safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Generally yes, especially in tourist areas. Some unwanted male attention in more conservative neighborhoods, but violent incidents are very rare.

faq.q: Is the Grand Bazaar safe?
faq.a: Yes, physically safe. But it's the prime pickpocket zone — keep valuables in front pockets and bags zipped. Vendors are persistent but not threatening.

faq.q: Should I worry about terrorism in Istanbul?
faq.a: Istanbul has experienced attacks in the past, but security has been significantly increased. Tourist areas are well-monitored. The risk is present but low.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport?
faq.a: Yes, very safe. The metro, tram, and ferries are clean and well-monitored. Rush hour can be extremely crowded — watch for pickpockets.

cities.name: Marseille
cities.summary: Marseille is France's grittiest major city — and its most authentic. The Vieux-Port is beautiful, the bouillabaisse legendary, and the Calanques national park stunning. It has real crime issues that tourists should be aware of, but the rewards are worth the extra vigilance.
cities.quickVerdict: Rougher than Paris or Nice but more authentic. Vieux-Port and La Joliette are tourist-friendly. Northern neighborhoods have serious crime. Don't flash valuables. The Calanques are paradise.

neighborhoods.name: Vieux-Port
neighborhoods.description: Old Port. Restaurants, MuCEM museum. Tourist center. Safe and bustling.

neighborhoods.name: Le Panier
neighborhoods.description: Oldest neighborhood. Colorful, artsy. Fine during day. Less lit at night.

neighborhoods.name: La Joliette / Euromed
neighborhoods.description: Redeveloped docks. Les Terrasses du Port mall. Modern and safe.

neighborhoods.name: Cours Julien
neighborhoods.description: Alternative culture, street art. Bars and cafés. Fine during day.

neighborhoods.name: La Canebière (north end)
neighborhoods.description: Main boulevard deteriorates northward. More caution needed.

neighborhoods.name: Northern Quarters (13e-16e)
neighborhoods.description: Drug trafficking, gang activity. Serious crime. Absolutely avoid.

scams.name: Phone Snatching
scams.description: Phones grabbed from hands, especially near train station and northern areas.
scams.howToAvoid: Phone in pocket. Don't walk and text.

scams.name: Pickpocketing
scams.description: Metro, markets, and crowded areas.
scams.howToAvoid: Bags in front. Money belt for valuables.

scams.name: Fake Petitions
scams.description: Same as Paris — distraction technique.
scams.howToAvoid: Ignore. Keep walking.

scams.name: Car Break-ins
scams.description: Rental cars targeted at scenic stops and beaches.
scams.howToAvoid: Nothing visible in car. Use paid parking.

soloFemale.overview: More challenging than Paris or Nice. Manageable in tourist areas. The Calanques are beautiful for hiking. Marseille has character that rewards the adventurous.
tips: Vieux-Port and La Joliette most comfortable
More edge than other French cities
Calanques hiking is spectacular
Metro safe during day
Don't walk to northern areas
Bouillabaisse at Vieux-Port restaurants is a must

nightSafety.overview: Vieux-Port has restaurants. Cours Julien has alternative nightlife. The city quiets down earlier than Paris.
tips: Vieux-Port restaurants safe
Cours Julien bar scene
Don't walk far at night
Metro closes early — Uber after
Northern neighborhoods dangerous at night
Marseille has less late-night culture than Paris

transport.metro: Two metro lines. Clean, safe during day.
transport.rideshare: Uber works.
transport.taxis: Metered and generally reliable.
transport.tips: Metro and walking in center. Uber at night. TGV to Paris 3 hours.

customs: Marseillais are proudly different from Parisians
Bouillabaisse is sacred — don't insult it
Tipping: round up, 5-10% at restaurants
Football (OM) is religion — wear the right colors
French manners matter — always say bonjour
The accent is thick — even French speakers struggle

health.overview: La Timone hospital. Good healthcare with EU EHIC.
health.water: Tap water safe.
health.vaccinations: None required.
health.altitude: Sea level. Mediterranean climate. Mistral wind is intense.

faq.q: Safe?
faq.a: Tourist areas fine. Northern neighborhoods have serious crime.

faq.q: Is Marseille worth visiting?
faq.a: Absolutely. Most authentic major French city. Calanques are paradise.

faq.q: Rougher than Paris?
faq.a: Yes. More edge, more authentic. Different experience entirely.

faq.q: Bouillabaisse?
faq.a: Iconic. Budget €50-70 at a good restaurant.

faq.q: Calanques?
faq.a: Stunning hiking. Easily accessible from city center. Free entry but reservation needed in summer.

cities.name: Naples
cities.summary: Naples is raw, intense, and utterly authentic Italy. The best pizza on Earth. Pompeii and the Amalfi Coast are nearby. It's grittier than Rome or Florence — motorbike theft and petty crime are real. But the food, history, and energy are unmatched.
cities.quickVerdict: Grittier than northern Italy but incredibly rewarding. Centro Storico and waterfront are fine with awareness. Motorbike snatching is the main risk. The pizza is genuinely life-changing. Pompeii is a must.

neighborhoods.name: Centro Storico
neighborhoods.description: Historic heart. Spaccanapoli, churches, street food. Alive and chaotic. Watch bags.

neighborhoods.name: Chiaia / Lungomare
neighborhoods.description: Waterfront promenade. Upscale shops and restaurants. Safe and pleasant.

neighborhoods.name: Vomero
neighborhoods.description: Hilltop residential. Castel Sant'Elmo views. Safe and local.

neighborhoods.name: Quartieri Spagnoli
neighborhoods.description: Narrow streets, laundry lines, authentic. Fine during day. Riskier at night.

neighborhoods.name: Stazione Centrale area
neighborhoods.description: Around main train station. Pickpockets and street crime. Transit through.

neighborhoods.name: Scampia / Secondigliano
neighborhoods.description: Camorra territory. Extremely dangerous. Absolutely avoid.

scams.name: Motorbike Snatching
scams.description: Bags, phones, necklaces grabbed by scooter riders at speed.
scams.howToAvoid: Cross-body bag on building side. Don't use phone near curb.

scams.name: Parking 'Attendants'
scams.description: Unofficial people demand payment for watching your car.
scams.howToAvoid: Pay €1-2 to avoid trouble. Use official garages.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Meters 'broken' or long routes.
scams.howToAvoid: Fixed rates exist: station to center €9-12. Insist on meter.

scams.name: Counterfeit Goods
scams.description: Street sellers with fake designer goods.
scams.howToAvoid: Buying is technically illegal for the buyer too.

soloFemale.overview: Manageable for solo women with awareness. Naples is loud and chaotic but Italian culture respects women more than reputation suggests. Chiaia is most comfortable.
tips: Chiaia is most comfortable area
Centro Storico fine during day
Solo pizza dining is normal — Naples invented it
Watch bags constantly
Motorbike snatching targets women's bags
Pompeii easy day trip by Circumvesuviana train

nightSafety.overview: Chiaia waterfront lovely evenings. Centro Storico has bars. Quartieri Spagnoli atmospheric but watch bags. Station area not safe at night.
tips: Chiaia waterfront safe evenings
Centro Storico bars fun but watch bags
Quartieri Spagnoli atmospheric — main streets only
Station area — avoid at night
Taxi/Uber for transport
Naples nightlife is vibrant but contained

transport.metro: Metro Line 1 has stunning art stations. Line 2 connects station. Both useful.
transport.rideshare: Uber limited. Free Now app works.
transport.taxis: White licensed taxis. Insist on meter or fixed rate.
transport.tips: Metro plus walking. Circumvesuviana train for Pompeii and Sorrento (watch bags on train).

customs: Pizza in Naples is serious — don't ask for pineapple
Coffee is an art — espresso at the bar standing
Tipping: €1-2 at restaurants, round up
Neapolitans are loud and passionate — it's normal
Don't compare Naples unfavorably to Rome
Gesticulation is communication

health.overview: Ospedale Cardarelli. Good healthcare with EU EHIC.
health.water: Tap water safe.
health.vaccinations: None required.
health.altitude: Sea level. Vesuvius is an active volcano — know evacuation routes.

faq.q: Safe?
faq.a: Grittier than northern Italy. Safe with awareness. Bag snatching is real.

faq.q: Best pizza?
faq.a: Da Michele, Sorbillo, Di Matteo, Starita. Can't go wrong.

faq.q: Pompeii?
faq.a: Easy day trip. Circumvesuviana train. Watch bags on train.

faq.q: Amalfi Coast?
faq.a: Accessible by bus, ferry, or car from Naples.

faq.q: Is the Camorra a concern?
faq.a: Not for tourists. Stay out of Scampia/Secondigliano.

cities.name: Tbilisi
cities.summary: Tbilisi is one of the safest capital cities in the world and one of the best-value destinations. Incredible food, legendary wine, ancient history, and the warmest hospitality. Crime against tourists is extremely rare. The biggest risk is eating and drinking too much.
cities.quickVerdict: Extremely safe. Walk the old town at 3am without worry. The food and wine are extraordinary. Incredibly affordable. One of the most underrated cities in the world.

neighborhoods.name: Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Cobblestone streets, sulfur baths, Narikala Fortress. Beautiful and very safe.

neighborhoods.name: Rustaveli Avenue
neighborhoods.description: Main boulevard. Museums, theaters, Opera House. Feels grand and safe.

neighborhoods.name: Vera / Mtatsminda
neighborhoods.description: Charming residential. Funicular, cafés. Very safe.

neighborhoods.name: Fabrika / Marjanishvili
neighborhoods.description: Creative hub. Hostels, restaurants, bars. Safe and trendy.

neighborhoods.name: Vake
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential. Parks, restaurants. Very safe.

neighborhoods.name: Gldani / outskirts
neighborhoods.description: Soviet-era suburbs. No tourist interest. Still relatively safe.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Some drivers inflate prices, especially from airport.
scams.howToAvoid: Use Bolt or Yandex Go. Airport to center should be 25-35 GEL.

scams.name: Bar Overcharging
scams.description: Very rare, unlike Istanbul. Some tourist restaurants mark up.
scams.howToAvoid: Check prices before ordering.

scams.name: Money Exchange
scams.description: Some exchangers give bad rates.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs or established exchange offices.

scams.name: Fake Wine
scams.description: Some tourist shops sell poor quality wine as premium.
scams.howToAvoid: Buy from reputable wine shops or supermarkets.

soloFemale.overview: One of the best cities in the world for solo travelers, especially women. Georgians are genuinely protective of guests. The hospitality culture is legendary — you're a guest of the country.
tips: Extremely safe for solo women
Georgian hospitality is legendary — expect to be fed
Old town and Fabrika area perfect for meeting travelers
Wine is everywhere and amazing
Walking is safe day and night
Learn 'gamarjoba' (hello) and 'madloba' (thank you)

nightSafety.overview: Tbilisi is very safe at night. The Old Town is atmospheric. Fabrika area has bars. Bassiani club is a world-famous techno venue. Walking home at 3am is normal and safe.
tips: Old Town safe late at night
Fabrika area has great nightlife
Walking home late is normal and safe
Bassiani/Khidi for electronic music
Georgian wine flows freely
Drivers tend to speed — watch crossing streets

transport.metro: Two metro lines. Clean, safe, cheap. Soviet-era stations.
transport.rideshare: Bolt and Yandex Go work well. Very cheap.
transport.taxis: Use apps. Very affordable.
transport.tips: Bolt for most transport. Metro for some routes. Marshrutkas (minibuses) for day trips.

customs: Georgians are famously hospitable — supra (feast) culture is real
Toast culture is elaborate — the tamada (toastmaster) leads
Don't refuse food or drink — it's deeply disrespectful
Orthodox churches require modest dress — scarves for women
Wine is 8,000 years old here — respect the tradition
Learn a few Georgian words — they'll love you for it

health.overview: Todua Clinic and MediClub Georgia are good.
health.water: Tap water safe in Tbilisi.
