neighborhoods.description: The main archaeological site including the Treasury, Monastery, and Royal Tombs is heavily monitored by tourist police with visible security presence. Well-maintained paths and facilities make this the safest area, though visitors should watch for uneven terrain and stay on marked trails.

neighborhoods.name: Wadi Musa Town Center
neighborhoods.description: The gateway town to Petra offers numerous hotels, restaurants, and shops. Generally safe during daylight hours with good tourist infrastructure, though visitors should be aware of persistent vendors and negotiate prices clearly to avoid overcharging.

neighborhoods.name: Petra Visitor Center & Entrance
neighborhoods.description: The official entry point is well-organized with security screenings and tourist police presence. Modern facilities include restrooms, shops, and information services. Primary concerns involve ticket scams and unofficial guide harassment.

neighborhoods.name: The Siq Canyon Entrance
neighborhoods.description: The dramatic 1.2km gorge leading to the Treasury can be crowded and narrow. Flash flood risk exists during rainy season with early warning systems in place. Animal handlers may pressure tourists for rides, and path lighting is limited.

neighborhoods.name: Little Petra (Al-Beidha)
neighborhoods.description: This smaller Nabatean site north of main Petra offers fewer crowds but also less security presence. Access roads can be challenging, and visitors may encounter more aggressive local guides. Best visited with organized tours or trusted local contacts.

neighborhoods.name: Remote Hiking Trails & Viewpoints
neighborhoods.description: High-altitude viewpoints and off-the-beaten-path trails like Al-Khubtha offer spectacular views but pose safety risks including rockfall hazards, extreme heat exposure, unmarked paths, and limited rescue access. Romance scam reports from these isolated areas advise extra caution.

scams.name: Fake Photo Spot Entry Fees
scams.description: Locals charge unofficial entry fees for Treasury viewpoints and cliff-top photo spots, claiming these areas require special permission or guides. They can be aggressive in enforcement and may demand 20-50 JOD per person.
scams.howToAvoid: All areas within Petra Archaeological Park are included in your entry ticket. Walk confidently past anyone demanding extra fees and report persistent harassment to tourist police stationed throughout the site.

scams.name: Free Ride Turned Expensive
scams.description: Animal handlers offer free horse, donkey or camel rides then demand large payments afterward, claiming the free portion was only to a specific point. They may become confrontational when payment is refused.
scams.howToAvoid: Negotiate all terms clearly before accepting any rides. If you hear free, ask exactly what is included and what is not. Better to pay upfront or decline entirely. Use only official animal services through the visitor center.

scams.name: Romance Scammer Bedouins
scams.description: Charming local men, often wearing traditional dress and eyeliner, target solo female travelers with romantic advances, invitations to private areas, and stories designed to elicit sympathy and money transfers after tourists return home.
scams.howToAvoid: Be extremely wary of invitations to private caves, remote hiking, or personal meetings. Never share personal contact information or send money after your trip. Stay in populated areas and trust your instincts if something feels too good to be true.

scams.name: Overpriced Unofficial Guides
scams.description: Self-proclaimed guides approach tourists claiming official status and offering exclusive experiences at inflated prices. They may be persistent and claim tourist police support their activities.
scams.howToAvoid: Use only licensed guides arranged through the official visitor center or reputable tour companies. Licensed guides carry official identification and wear recognizable uniforms. Politely decline unsolicited guide offers and walk toward tourist police if pressured.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel to Petra is generally manageable with proper precautions, though the current security climate requires extra vigilance. The site has good tourist infrastructure and police presence, but cultural differences and recent reports of harassment in remote areas mean extra caution is warranted. Local culture is conservative but respectful, and most interactions will be positive.
tips: Dress very modestly covering shoulders, arms, and legs to show respect and avoid unwanted attention
Stay on main tourist trails and avoid isolated areas, especially with unmarked local guides
Consider wearing a wedding ring (real or fake) to signal unavailability and respectability
Book accommodations with good reviews and verified tourist police recommendations
Join group tours when possible rather than exploring completely alone

nightSafety.overview: Petra's main archaeological site closes at sunset for safety reasons, and unauthorized nighttime presence is strictly prohibited with risk of arrest. The official Petra by Night experience offers safe, supervised access on select evenings with security escorts. Wadi Musa town has basic nightlife but limited after-dark activities, with most restaurants and cafes closing early.
tips: Only visit Petra after dark through official Petra by Night tours with proper authorization
Stay in well-lit central areas of Wadi Musa and avoid walking alone after sunset
Use ride-sharing apps or hotel-arranged taxis rather than hailing random vehicles
Keep emergency numbers saved and inform someone of your whereabouts
Avoid isolated areas and stick to main tourist zones where security presence is maintained

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Jordan. Petra is accessed via Wadi Musa town with no rail connections.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Careem operate in major Jordanian cities but have limited presence in Wadi Musa. Local taxi apps like Petra Ride may be available but coverage is inconsistent.
transport.taxis: Regular taxis are available but often lack meters outside Amman. Negotiate fares in advance and expect to pay 2-5 JOD for local trips. Hotel-arranged taxis are generally safer and more reliable.
transport.tips: JETT buses provide reliable intercity service from Amman to Petra. For airport transfers and day trips, pre-arranged private drivers through hotels offer the most convenience and safety, though costs range from 140-350 USD depending on distance and duration.

customs: Dress modestly with shoulders and knees covered at all times, both men and women
Remove shoes when entering traditional homes and avoid showing shoe soles
Accept offered tea or coffee as a sign of respect, even if briefly
Use right hand for eating, greeting, and passing objects
Stand when elders enter the room and show deference in conversation

health.overview: Jordan has good healthcare infrastructure with modern hospitals in major cities, though services become limited in rural areas around Petra. Private medical facilities offer high standards but can be expensive for foreigners.
health.water: Bottled water is recommended for drinking. Tap water is generally safe for brushing teeth but may cause stomach upset. Most hotels provide complimentary bottled water.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Jordan. Standard travel vaccines (hepatitis A/B, typhoid, routine immunizations) are recommended. Check CDC guidelines before travel.
health.altitude: Petra sits at 800-1000m elevation with hot, dry desert climate. Dehydration and heat exhaustion are primary health risks. Temperatures can exceed 40°C in summer, dropping significantly at night in winter.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Amman: +962 6 590 6000 (24hr emergency). Due to March 2026 ordered departure, embassy services are limited. US citizens should call +1-202-501-4444 from abroad for assistance.

faq.q: Is Petra safe for tourists?
faq.a: Petra remains relatively safe for tourists with proper precautions, though the March 2026 US State Department Level 3 advisory recommends reconsidering travel due to regional tensions. The site has strong security measures and tourist police presence, but visitors should stay informed of changing conditions and avoid non-essential travel during current instability.

faq.q: Is Petra safe at night?
faq.a: Petra closes at sunset and unauthorized nighttime access is prohibited with arrest risks. The official Petra by Night experience operates on select evenings with security escorts and is generally safe. Wadi Musa town has limited nightlife and visitors should stick to well-lit areas near hotels after dark.

faq.q: Is Petra safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travel to Petra requires extra caution but is manageable with proper planning. Dress modestly, stay on main tourist areas, and be very wary of romantic advances or invitations to isolated areas. Consider joining group tours and stay at well-reviewed accommodations with tourist police recommendations.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Petra?
faq.a: Avoid remote hiking trails without proper guides, unauthorized viewpoints where locals demand fees, and private invitations to caves or isolated areas. The US State Department specifically warns against certain areas of Ma'an Governorate where Petra is located due to terrorism and crime risks.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Petra?
faq.a: Public transport to Petra is limited. JETT buses from Amman are reliable and safe. Local transport around Wadi Musa includes taxis without meters, so negotiate fares upfront. Private drivers arranged through hotels are safest but more expensive. Ride-sharing apps have limited coverage in the area.

cities.name: Wadi Rum
cities.summary: Wadi Rum is Jordan's stunning desert wilderness that remains generally safe for tourists despite regional tensions. While the desert environment poses natural challenges including flash floods and extreme temperatures, violent crime is rare and the Bedouin communities are renowned for their hospitality toward visitors.
cities.quickVerdict: Wadi Rum offers a relatively safe desert experience with excellent local hospitality, though visitors should be aware of current regional tensions that have prompted a US Level 3 travel advisory for Jordan. The main risks come from natural hazards like flash floods and extreme weather rather than crime. Tourist scams exist but are manageable with proper precautions. The area benefits from tourism police presence and well-established camp operators, making it accessible for solo travelers including women.

neighborhoods.name: Wadi Rum Village
neighborhoods.description: The main settlement with shops, visitor center, and official entry point. Well-patrolled by tourism police with established infrastructure for visitors.

neighborhoods.name: Central Desert Camps
neighborhoods.description: Licensed Bedouin camps in the protected area core with experienced guides and established safety protocols for tourists.

neighborhoods.name: Northern Desert Routes
neighborhoods.description: More remote areas requiring experienced guides and proper preparation. Risk of getting lost without local knowledge.

neighborhoods.name: Eastern Border Areas
neighborhoods.description: Areas near Saudi border with restricted access and potential security concerns. Avoided by most tourist operations.

neighborhoods.name: Flash Flood Zones
neighborhoods.description: Wadis and low-lying areas prone to sudden flooding during rare but intense rainstorms. Extremely dangerous during weather events.

neighborhoods.name: Rock Climbing Areas
neighborhoods.description: Popular climbing spots with natural risks from rockfall and extreme temperatures. Require proper equipment and experience.

scams.name: Fake Wadi Rum Tours
scams.description: Operators in Petra or Aqaba offering tours to areas outside the protected area, claiming it's authentic Wadi Rum.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book through licensed operators and ensure tours enter through the official Visitor Center at Wadi Rum Village.

scams.name: Fraudulent Entry Tickets
scams.description: Fake entrance tickets sold by unauthorized individuals at makeshift gates before the official visitor center.
scams.howToAvoid: Purchase tickets only at the official Wadi Rum Visitor Center or use the Jordan Pass. Verify tickets have official stamps.

scams.name: Overpriced Jeep Tours
scams.description: Desert camps charging inflated prices for activities after arrival, particularly for jeep safaris and camel rides.
scams.howToAvoid: Book package deals in advance that include all activities, or verify official pricing at the Visitor Center before agreeing to any tours.

scams.name: Transport Price Gouging
scams.description: Taxi drivers charging excessive rates for transfers to and from Wadi Rum, especially during peak season.
scams.howToAvoid: Arrange transport through your accommodation or use established bus services. Agree on prices before starting any journey.

soloFemale.overview: Wadi Rum is considered safe for solo female travelers with thousands visiting annually. The Bedouin culture emphasizes protecting female guests, and established camps maintain professional standards. Most solo women report positive experiences, though cultural awareness and modest dress are important.
tips: Choose reputable licensed camps with good reviews from other female travelers
Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees out of respect for local customs
Inform your camp of hiking plans and stick to guided activities rather than exploring alone

nightSafety.overview: Night safety in Wadi Rum is generally excellent due to the remote location and strong Bedouin hospitality traditions. The desert environment is peaceful with minimal crime risks. Most safety concerns relate to natural hazards rather than human threats.
tips: Stay close to your camp after dark as the desert can be disorienting
Bring warm clothing as temperatures drop significantly at night
Enjoy stargazing activities but always go with guides who know the terrain

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Wadi Rum. The area is accessed via the Visitor Center and requires 4WD vehicles or organized tours for desert exploration.
transport.rideshare: Rideshare apps like Uber and Careem are not available in Wadi Rum. Transport is arranged through camps, tour operators, or private taxis.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis operate between major cities and Wadi Rum Village. Expect to pay 25-45 JOD from Aqaba or Petra respectively. Arrange through hotels for best rates.
transport.tips: Book transport in advance during peak season and always use licensed operators for safety and reliability.

customs: Remove shoes when entering Bedouin tents and accept hospitality graciously
Dress modestly with covered shoulders and knees, especially when interacting with local families
Respect prayer times and avoid loud activities during Islamic prayer periods
Photography of local people should be done with permission, particularly women
Tipping 10-15% is appreciated for guides and camp staff but not mandatory

health.overview: Medical facilities in Wadi Rum are limited with the nearest hospitals in Aqaba 60km away. Basic first aid is available at camps but serious emergencies require evacuation to urban centers.
health.water: Bottled water is readily available at camps and should be consumed exclusively. Desert camps provide water but verify it's properly sourced.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccines recommended including Hepatitis A and Tetanus. Rabies vaccination advised for extended stays due to desert dogs.
health.altitude: Desert climate with extreme temperature variations from 45°C days to 5°C nights. Sun protection and hydration essential. Flash flood risk during rare storms.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Amman: +962 6 590 6500 (Al-Umayyaween Street, Abdoun, Amman). Note: Non-emergency personnel departed March 2026 due to regional tensions.

faq.q: Is Wadi Rum safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Wadi Rum is generally safe for tourists with low crime rates and strong Bedouin hospitality traditions. Current regional tensions have raised the US travel advisory to Level 3, but tourist areas remain operational with proper security measures.

faq.q: Is Wadi Rum safe at night?
faq.a: Wadi Rum is very safe at night with peaceful desert conditions and protective camp environments. The main concerns are natural hazards like temperature drops and disorientation rather than crime or security issues.

faq.q: Is Wadi Rum safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Wadi Rum is considered safe for solo female travelers. Thousands visit annually with positive experiences. Choose reputable camps, dress modestly, and follow local customs for the best experience.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Wadi Rum?
faq.a: Avoid unlicensed camps outside the protected area, eastern border regions near Saudi Arabia, and low-lying wadis during weather warnings. Always use official entry points and licensed tour operators.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport to Wadi Rum?
faq.a: Public bus options are limited but safe. Most visitors use JETT buses to nearby cities then arrange taxi transfers. Private transfers through licensed operators are recommended for convenience and reliability.

cities.name: Jerusalem
cities.summary: Jerusalem offers rich history and culture but faces significant security challenges with ongoing regional tensions and security incidents. While day-to-day crime is relatively low, the complex political situation requires heightened awareness and preparedness for emergency situations.
cities.quickVerdict: Jerusalem remains accessible to tourists but requires careful planning due to heightened security concerns. The US State Department currently advises reconsidering travel to Israel, with specific restrictions in some areas. Tourist zones like the Western Wall and major hotels continue operating, but visitors should stay informed about current conditions, have evacuation plans, and be prepared for potential security restrictions. The city's excellent healthcare system and strong security presence provide important safety infrastructure, but the unpredictable nature of regional tensions means situations can change rapidly.

neighborhoods.name: West Jerusalem (City Center)
neighborhoods.description: Main tourist and commercial area with good security presence. Hotels, restaurants, and attractions continue operating normally. Regular police patrols and established emergency procedures.

neighborhoods.name: Jewish Quarter (Old City)
neighborhoods.description: Historic area around Western Wall with heavy security presence. Access may be restricted during security incidents. Tourist police and clear evacuation routes available.

neighborhoods.name: Mahane Yehuda Market Area
neighborhoods.description: Vibrant market and nightlife district. Well-patrolled area with security checkpoints. Popular with both locals and tourists, offering good situational awareness.

neighborhoods.name: East Jerusalem
neighborhoods.description: Higher tension area with potential for unrest. US Embassy restricts staff travel here. Visitors should exercise extreme caution and consider avoiding non-essential visits.

neighborhoods.name: Mount of Olives
neighborhoods.description: Tourist site with panoramic views but higher security risk. Reports of harassment and theft. Best visited with organized tours during daylight hours only.

neighborhoods.name: German Colony/First Station
neighborhoods.description: Modern, upscale neighborhood with restaurants and cultural venues. Well-maintained area with good lighting and regular foot traffic providing natural security.

scams.name: Menu Switching Scam
scams.description: Restaurants show reasonable prices for items like falafel but bring expensive platters without asking. Bill manipulation at restaurants near tourist sites.
scams.howToAvoid: Clearly specify what you want to order, confirm prices verbally, and check your receipt carefully before paying. Only pay after receiving the correct item.

scams.name: Red String Blessing Scam
scams.description: People approach tourists near Western Wall offering to tie red strings and give blessings, then demand payment afterward.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline any unsolicited religious items or services. Keep walking and don't accept anything you didn't specifically request.

scams.name: Fake Photography Accident
scams.description: Locals ask tourists to take their photo, then deliberately drop the camera and demand compensation for damage while claiming tourist broke it.
scams.howToAvoid: Refuse requests to take photos for strangers unless you're completely confident they're genuine tourists. Trust your instincts and walk away if something feels off.

scams.name: ATM Skimming and Assistance
scams.description: Criminals offer help at ATMs to avoid fees while secretly using card skimmers to steal information and watch PIN entry.
scams.howToAvoid: Never accept help at ATMs, cover your PIN entry with your hand, and use ATMs inside banks when possible. If card isn't working, go inside the bank for assistance.

soloFemale.overview: Jerusalem is generally considered safe for solo female travelers, with many women reporting feeling comfortable walking around during the day and evening in tourist areas. The strong security presence and low violent crime rates contribute to overall safety. However, the current regional situation requires extra vigilance, and some areas like East Jerusalem should be avoided.
tips: Stay in central accommodations near major tourist areas with good security
Join group tours or walking tours to meet other travelers and have local guidance
Dress modestly when visiting religious sites - cover shoulders and knees
Be aware of cultural differences in Arab-majority areas where you may receive more attention
Download safety apps and keep emergency numbers handy
Inform someone of your whereabouts and check in regularly
Trust your instincts and avoid isolated areas, especially after dark

nightSafety.overview: Jerusalem at night offers beautiful illuminated sites and active nightlife in certain areas, but requires careful area selection. West Jerusalem, particularly around Mahane Yehuda Market and the city center, has vibrant evening scenes with good security. The Old City becomes very quiet after dark with limited lighting, while some areas should be completely avoided at night.
tips: Stick to well-lit, populated areas like Machane Yehuda Market for nightlife
The Old City has limited lighting and uneven surfaces - exercise extreme caution
Use registered taxis or rideshare apps rather than walking long distances
Stay aware of your surroundings and trust local security guidance
Keep emergency numbers programmed in your phone
Inform your hotel of your evening plans and expected return time

transport.metro: Jerusalem Light Rail operates a single red line from Mount Herzl to Pisgat Ze'ev, passing major tourist sites. Trains run every 6-8 minutes during peak hours. Service stops during Shabbat (Friday evening to Saturday evening). Use Rav Kav cards or mobile apps for payment. Generally safe and reliable with security presence.
transport.rideshare: Gett is the main rideshare app in Israel, widely used and reliable. Service may be limited during security incidents or emergency situations. Drivers are generally trustworthy, and the app provides safety features including driver details and trip tracking.
transport.taxis: Taxis are abundant but drivers may overcharge tourists. Always insist on using the meter or agree on fare beforehand. Licensed taxis are safer than unlicensed ones. During security situations, taxi availability may be reduced. Expect higher rates during emergencies or high-demand periods.
transport.tips: Public transport stops during Shabbat. Keep alternative transportation options available. During security alerts, services may be suspended temporarily. Download offline maps as backup navigation.

customs: Respect religious diversity - Jerusalem is holy to Judaism, Christianity, and Islam
Dress modestly at religious sites (cover shoulders and knees), men should wear head covering
Shabbat observance affects city operations from Friday evening to Saturday evening
Israelis value directness in communication - this is cultural, not rudeness
Gift-giving often uses multiples of 18 (chai - meaning life in Hebrew)
Physical contact like handshakes is common, but some Orthodox Jews avoid opposite-gender contact
Tipping is customary in restaurants (10-15%) and for various services

health.overview: Israel has an excellent healthcare system with modern facilities and well-trained medical professionals. Healthcare quality meets or exceeds Western standards, with 73% of people satisfied with healthcare availability. Jerusalem has several major hospitals including Hadassah Ein Kerem and Shaare Zedek.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Jerusalem and Israel. The quality meets international standards and is regularly tested. Bottled water is widely available but not necessary for health reasons.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for travel to Israel. Routine vaccinations (MMR, DPT, flu, etc.) should be up to date. COVID-19 vaccination requirements may apply depending on current policies.
health.altitude: Jerusalem sits at about 760 meters (2,500 feet) above sea level. Most travelers experience no altitude-related issues. Climate is Mediterranean with hot, dry summers and mild winters. Summer temperatures can exceed 30°C (86°F), so stay hydrated.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Jerusalem: +972-2-630-4000 (outside Israel) / 02-630-4000 (within Israel). Embassy Branch Office Tel Aviv also available. Services may be limited during security situations. Register with STEP program for emergency updates.

faq.q: Is Jerusalem safe for tourists?
faq.a: Jerusalem can be safe for tourists but requires careful planning due to ongoing regional tensions. Tourist areas continue operating with strong security presence, but visitors should stay informed about current conditions and be prepared for potential restrictions or evacuations.

faq.q: Is Jerusalem safe at night?
faq.a: Nighttime safety varies significantly by area. West Jerusalem and tourist zones like Mahane Yehuda Market are generally safe with good lighting and security. However, avoid East Jerusalem and isolated areas after dark. The Old City becomes very quiet with limited lighting.

faq.q: Is Jerusalem safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travelers generally report feeling safe in Jerusalem's main tourist areas during normal times. The current security situation requires extra precautions, but many women travel successfully by staying in central areas, joining groups, and following security guidance.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Jerusalem?
faq.a: Currently avoid East Jerusalem due to US Embassy restrictions on staff travel. The Mount of Olives requires caution due to harassment reports. During security incidents, access to Old City areas may be restricted. Stay updated on current conditions and follow official guidance.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Jerusalem?
faq.a: Public transportation is generally safe and reliable under normal circumstances. The light rail has security presence and serves major tourist areas. However, services may be suspended during security incidents. Have backup transportation options and stay informed about service disruptions.

cities.name: Beirut
cities.summary: Beirut faces extreme security challenges with ongoing regional conflicts, Level 4 travel advisory, and US Embassy evacuation orders. The city experiences terrorism threats, political instability, and deteriorating infrastructure amid a collapsed economy.
cities.quickVerdict: Beirut is currently extremely dangerous for tourists with the US State Department maintaining a Level 4 Do Not Travel advisory. The February 2026 evacuation of non-essential embassy personnel amid regional warfare, terrorism threats, and complete healthcare system breakdown makes travel highly inadvisable. Those who ignore warnings face risks of being trapped with no consular support.

neighborhoods.name: Hamra & Ras Beirut
neighborhoods.description: Traditional tourist district near American University of Beirut. While historically safer, current security situation makes even central areas dangerous with regular checkpoints and military presence.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Beirut
neighborhoods.description: Central business district with government buildings and financial centers. Heavily secured but prime target for political demonstrations and potential terrorist attacks.

neighborhoods.name: Gemmayzeh & Mar Mikhael
neighborhoods.description: Former nightlife district still recovering from 2020 port explosion. Infrastructure damage persists and area remains vulnerable to security incidents.

neighborhoods.name: Achrafieh
neighborhoods.description: Upscale Christian neighborhood with embassies and international organizations. Despite affluence, faces same regional security threats as rest of city.

neighborhoods.name: Dahieh (Southern Suburbs)
neighborhoods.description: Under Hezbollah control and specifically mentioned in US warnings to avoid. Scene of recent Israeli airstrikes and ongoing military operations.

neighborhoods.name: Airport Area
neighborhoods.description: Vulnerable corridor between city and international airport. Frequent target of road blockades, demonstrations, and security incidents affecting evacuation routes.

scams.name: Fake Tour Guides
scams.description: Unauthorized individuals posing as official tour guides, especially targeting tourists at historical sites and hotels.
scams.howToAvoid: Only use guides arranged through reputable hotels or agencies. Verify credentials and agree on prices beforehand.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Scams
scams.description: Street money changers offering unfavorable rates or counterfeit bills amid economic crisis and currency instability.
scams.howToAvoid: Use only official banks or authorized exchange bureaus. Avoid street exchanges entirely.

scams.name: Official Fee Extortion
scams.description: Corrupt officials or fake authorities demanding bribes or inventing violations, particularly at checkpoints.
scams.howToAvoid: Carry proper documentation, remain polite but firm, and ask for official receipts for any payments.

scams.name: ATM Distraction Theft
scams.description: Groups creating distractions while accessing ATMs to steal cards or cash, especially in crowded areas.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks when possible, stay alert, and shield your PIN entry.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel to Beirut is extremely dangerous given current security conditions. Even under normal circumstances, women face harassment and cultural restrictions. Current warfare and instability create additional severe risks including sexual assault and inability to access help.
tips: Do not travel to Lebanon as a solo female under current conditions
If already present, maintain constant communication with embassy and family
Avoid all public areas and maintain low profile
Dress very conservatively to avoid unwanted attention
Never travel alone outside secured accommodations

nightSafety.overview: Nighttime in Beirut is extremely dangerous with military curfews, active combat zones, and complete breakdown of normal policing. Even areas previously considered safe are now subject to airstrikes, military operations, and armed confrontations.
tips: Observe all curfews and military restrictions strictly
Stay indoors after dark unless absolutely necessary
Keep emergency supplies and communication devices charged
Monitor news constantly for evacuation orders
Have multiple escape routes planned from your location

transport.metro: Beirut has no metro system. Public buses operate sporadically due to fuel shortages and security concerns.
transport.rideshare: Limited availability due to economic crisis and driver safety concerns. Apps like Uber and Careem operate with restrictions.
transport.taxis: Traditional service taxis and private taxis available but drivers may refuse certain areas. Negotiate prices due to fuel costs.
transport.tips: All transport severely disrupted by ongoing conflict. Airport road subject to closure. Use only trusted drivers and avoid travel near military zones.

customs: Remove shoes when entering homes and some religious sites
Accept offers of coffee or tea as refusing is considered rude - it may be offered three times
Dress modestly especially when visiting religious areas or outside central Beirut
Greet with handshake and direct eye contact, or three kisses on alternating cheeks with close friends
Show respect for both Christian and Muslim religious practices as Lebanon is highly diverse

health.overview: Lebanon's healthcare system has completely collapsed due to economic crisis, ongoing conflict, and medical supply shortages. Many hospitals have closed or operate with severely limited capacity.
health.water: Tap water is unsafe to drink throughout Lebanon. Use bottled water for drinking, cooking, and teeth brushing. Infrastructure heavily damaged.
health.vaccinations: Consult travel medicine specialist before any travel. Routine vaccines plus Hepatitis A/B, Typhoid recommended. Medical care extremely limited if complications arise.
health.altitude: Beirut is at sea level. Mediterranean climate but current conditions include damaged infrastructure, power outages, and limited air conditioning during hot summers.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy services suspended. Emergency contact: +1-202-501-4444 (from abroad) or +1-888-407-4747 (from US/Canada). Embassy located Awkar, north of Beirut but staff evacuated.

faq.q: Is Beirut safe for tourists?
faq.a: No. The US State Department has issued a Level 4 Do Not Travel advisory with embassy personnel evacuation. Ongoing regional warfare, terrorism threats, and complete infrastructure breakdown make tourism extremely dangerous.

faq.q: Is Beirut safe at night?
faq.a: Absolutely not. Military curfews are in effect, active combat zones exist throughout the city, and normal policing has collapsed. Airstrikes and armed confrontations can occur without warning.

faq.q: Is Beirut safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Extremely dangerous. Current conflict conditions combined with cultural harassment issues create severe risks. Embassy cannot provide assistance and evacuation routes are limited.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Beirut?
faq.a: All areas are dangerous, but specifically avoid Dahieh southern suburbs (Hezbollah-controlled), airport road, government buildings, and anywhere near Syrian border. US Embassy warns against entire country.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Beirut?
faq.a: No reliable public transport exists due to fuel shortages and security concerns. Private transport also severely limited. Airport access frequently blocked by military operations.

cities.name: Kuwait City
cities.summary: Kuwait City faces significant safety challenges in early 2026 with ongoing regional tensions, a Level 3 US State Department travel advisory, and suspended US Embassy operations. While the city typically has low crime rates, current threats include missile attacks, landmines in northern areas, and general security risks from regional conflict.
cities.quickVerdict: Kuwait City is experiencing heightened security risks in 2026 due to regional armed conflict. The US State Department advises reconsidering travel, and the US Embassy has suspended operations. While crime rates are generally low, current threats include potential missile attacks, unexploded ordnance in certain areas, and disrupted commercial flights. Travelers should exercise extreme caution and consider postponing non-essential travel until conditions improve.

neighborhoods.name: Kuwait City Center
neighborhoods.description: Downtown area with government buildings and Kuwait Towers. Generally safer than outlying areas but subject to current regional security concerns and potential targeted attacks.

neighborhoods.name: Salmiya
neighborhoods.description: Coastal district popular with expatriates. Moderate safety but affected by current travel advisory and security situation.

neighborhoods.name: Hawalli
neighborhoods.description: Densely populated area with mixed security conditions. Exercise increased caution, especially for solo travelers.

neighborhoods.name: Ahmadi
neighborhoods.description: Oil industry area in the south. Generally stable but monitor security updates due to strategic importance.

neighborhoods.name: Jleeb Al-Shuyoukh
neighborhoods.description: High-crime area near Kuwait International Airport identified by Kuwaiti police. Avoid this neighborhood, especially at night, due to theft, violence, and unsafe conditions.

neighborhoods.name: Northern Border Areas
neighborhoods.description: Desert areas north of Mutla'a Ridge containing unexploded landmines and ordnance from the 1990 Gulf War. Extremely dangerous - do not travel to these areas under any circumstances.

scams.name: Cybercrime and Fake Government Sites
scams.description: Sophisticated scams using fake government websites and official-looking communications to steal personal data and money. Scammers may impersonate authorities or financial institutions.
scams.howToAvoid: Only use official .com.kw websites, verify all communications independently, never provide personal information via unsolicited calls or messages.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Taxi drivers may quote inflated prices or claim broken meters, especially to tourists and airport passengers.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ride-hailing apps like Careem, agree on fares upfront, or ensure the meter is working before starting your journey.

scams.name: Fake Traffic Fines via SMS
scams.description: Fraudulent text messages demanding immediate payment of traffic fines with threats of increased penalties.
scams.howToAvoid: Kuwait authorities only send traffic violations through the Sahel app, never via SMS. Do not click links or respond to such messages.

scams.name: Public Wi-Fi Exploitation
scams.description: Criminals use compromised public Wi-Fi networks to steal personal data, banking information, and monitor social media activity.
scams.howToAvoid: Avoid using public Wi-Fi for sensitive activities, use a VPN when necessary, and never install unknown apps or enter personal data on unsecured networks.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Kuwait City requires extra caution, especially given current security conditions. While violent crime against foreigners is uncommon, women may experience verbal harassment and should dress modestly. The current regional tensions add additional risks that female travelers should carefully consider.
tips: Dress conservatively covering shoulders and knees, carry a scarf for mosque visits
Avoid walking alone at night and use official transportation when possible
Be prepared for stares and occasional verbal harassment, especially in less touristy areas
Stay in well-populated areas and avoid isolated locations during current security situation

nightSafety.overview: Night safety is significantly compromised due to current regional tensions and security threats. Even in normal times, Kuwait City has limited pedestrian activity after dark as most residents drive. The current situation with potential missile threats and suspended embassy services makes nighttime travel inadvisable.
tips: Avoid unnecessary nighttime travel due to current security situation
If you must go out, use hotel transportation or pre-arranged trusted drivers
Stay in well-lit, populated areas and avoid industrial zones
Keep emergency contacts readily available and inform others of your whereabouts

transport.metro: Kuwait does not have an operational metro system. A metro project is under development but not yet available for public use.
transport.rideshare: Careem and Uber operate in Kuwait City and are generally the safest transportation option. Services may be disrupted during security alerts.
transport.taxis: Official taxis are available but not all have working meters. Agree on fares upfront and use registered taxis with official plates. Current security situation may affect availability.
transport.tips: Due to current regional tensions and airport closures, transportation options are severely limited. Overland routes to Saudi Arabia remain open but carry risks. Monitor security updates before traveling.

customs: Dress modestly in public - cover shoulders and knees, especially in religious sites
Show respect during prayer times when businesses may close briefly
Avoid public displays of affection between men and women
Use your right hand for eating and greeting, left hand is considered unclean
Accept offers of Arabic coffee and dates as refusing hospitality can be seen as rude
Remove shoes when entering homes or traditional dining areas

health.overview: Kuwait has excellent healthcare infrastructure with modern hospitals and clinics. Medical care is provided free to residents and available to tourists. Current security situation may affect access to some facilities.
health.water: Tap water is treated but bottled water is recommended for sensitive stomachs. Most hotels and restaurants use filtered water.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccinations recommended including MMR, Hepatitis A/B. Check with healthcare provider for current requirements. MERS-CoV risk exists - avoid contact with camels.
health.altitude: No altitude concerns. Extreme heat (40°C+) in summer requires precautions including hydration and avoiding midday sun. Dust storms can affect air quality.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy operations suspended as of March 5, 2026. US citizens should contact +1-202-501-4444 (from abroad) or +1-888-407-4747 (from US/Canada) for emergency assistance. Complete crisis intake form online for departure assistance.

faq.q: Is Kuwait City safe for tourists?
faq.a: Currently no - Kuwait City has a Level 3 travel advisory due to regional armed conflict, landmines, and security threats. The US Embassy has suspended operations. Reconsider travel until conditions improve.

faq.q: Is Kuwait City safe at night?
faq.a: No, especially during current security situation. Night travel is strongly discouraged due to potential missile attacks and limited emergency services. Even in normal times, night safety requires caution.

faq.q: Is Kuwait City safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Currently not recommended due to security situation. Even in stable times, solo female travel requires significant precautions including modest dress, avoiding isolation, and dealing with potential harassment.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Kuwait City?
faq.a: Avoid Jleeb Al-Shuyoukh (high crime), all areas north of Mutla'a Ridge (landmines), and currently all non-essential travel due to security threats. Monitor embassy updates for restricted areas.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Kuwait City?
faq.a: Limited options available and safety compromised by current situation. Public buses exist but ride-hailing apps are safer when operating. Commercial flights suspended due to missile threat - only overland travel to Saudi Arabia available.

cities.name: Manama
cities.summary: Manama is normally considered very safe for tourists with low crime rates and friendly locals, but the current regional conflict involving Iran and geopolitical tensions have significantly elevated security risks. The US State Department has issued a Level 3 travel advisory due to ongoing threats of missile and drone attacks.
cities.quickVerdict: Due to the current armed conflict between the United States and Iran and associated regional tensions, Manama faces elevated security risks including potential drone and missile attacks. While the city traditionally enjoys low crime rates and is normally safe for tourists, the exceptional circumstances require extreme caution. US and other Western citizens are advised to shelter in place or consider departure via land routes to Saudi Arabia, as the airport remains closed to commercial flights. Travel should be postponed unless absolutely essential.

neighborhoods.name: Juffair
neighborhoods.description: Popular expatriate area with numerous bars, restaurants, and international businesses. Generally considered very safe with high security presence and well-lit streets.

neighborhoods.name: Seef District
neighborhoods.description: Modern commercial and residential area with luxury hotels, malls, and upscale amenities. Well-patrolled and safe for tourists day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Diplomatic Area
neighborhoods.description: Financial district housing embassies, banks, and corporate headquarters. Heavily secured with excellent infrastructure and minimal crime risk.

neighborhoods.name: Manama Souq Area
neighborhoods.description: Historic market district where most petty crime occurs. Pickpocketing and smartphone theft are more common here, especially during busy periods.

neighborhoods.name: Bab Al Bahrain
neighborhoods.description: Historic gateway and popular tourist area with shops and restaurants. Generally safe but can be crowded with increased risk of minor scams and pickpocketing.

neighborhoods.name: Old Manama Districts
neighborhoods.description: Older residential areas that can be less safe at night with poor lighting and fewer security patrols. Best avoided after dark, especially for solo travelers.

scams.name: Taxi Meter Scam
scams.description: Some taxi drivers don't use meters or take longer routes to inflate fares, particularly targeting tourists unfamiliar with the city.
scams.howToAvoid: Always insist on using the meter or agree on a fixed fare beforehand. Use ride-hailing apps like Uber or Careem for transparent pricing.

scams.name: Fake Tour Guide
scams.description: Individuals pose as official guides offering tours at inflated prices or leading tourists to overpriced shops where they receive commissions.
scams.howToAvoid: Only use guides from reputable tour companies or hotels. Verify credentials and ask for identification before agreeing to any tours.

scams.name: Fake Jewelry in Souqs
scams.description: Vendors in markets may sell counterfeit gemstones or jewelry claiming they are genuine precious stones at seemingly bargain prices.
scams.howToAvoid: Buy only from established, reputable dealers. Be very wary of deals that seem too good to be true and consider having items authenticated.

scams.name: Overpriced Restaurant Menu
scams.description: Some tourist-area restaurants present menus with inflated prices compared to what locals pay, particularly in areas near major attractions.
scams.howToAvoid: Check prices clearly before ordering and choose restaurants that display their prices prominently. Ask locals for recommendations.

soloFemale.overview: Under normal circumstances, Manama is relatively safe for solo female travelers due to its progressive nature compared to other Gulf countries. However, current regional tensions and security alerts require extreme caution. Women should dress modestly, be mindful of local customs, and currently consider postponing travel due to ongoing security risks.
tips: Dress conservatively with shoulders and knees covered, especially in traditional areas
Stay in well-lit, populated areas and avoid isolated districts at night
Use reputable taxi services or ride-hailing apps rather than walking alone after dark

nightSafety.overview: Manama traditionally has good nighttime safety with low violent crime rates and active nightlife areas that are well-patrolled. However, current security alerts recommend staying indoors and avoiding unnecessary movement due to regional conflict risks including potential missile and drone attacks.
tips: Currently follow shelter-in-place guidelines issued by local authorities and embassies
Stay in secure accommodation and avoid unnecessary nighttime travel during current tensions
If you must travel, use licensed taxis or ride-hailing apps and stay in well-lit areas

transport.metro: Bahrain does not have a metro system. Public transport consists of a bus network operated by Bahrain Public Transport Company serving major areas including the airport and tourist attractions.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Careem operate throughout Manama and are considered safe and reliable options. Apps provide transparent pricing and tracking features for added security.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are identifiable by orange fenders and yellow license plates. Generally safe but always use meter or agree on fare beforehand. Some unlicensed taxis may overcharge.
transport.tips: Currently, commercial flights are suspended from Bahrain International Airport due to regional conflict. Land routes to Saudi Arabia remain open for those needing to depart. Use established ride-hailing services for local transport.

customs: Use your right hand for greetings, eating, and passing objects as the left hand is considered unclean
Dress modestly in public with shoulders and knees covered, especially in religious and traditional areas
Respect Islamic customs during prayer times and avoid eating or drinking in public during Ramadan daylight hours

health.overview: Bahrain has excellent healthcare infrastructure with modern hospitals and medical facilities. The country spends 4.5% of GDP on healthcare and provides high-quality services comparable to international standards.
health.water: Tap water is generally safe to drink as it meets quality standards, though many visitors prefer bottled water due to taste differences from mineral content.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for most travelers. Routine vaccinations should be up to date. Check CDC recommendations for any recent health alerts.
health.altitude: Sea-level location with desert climate. Summer temperatures can be extreme (over 40°C/104°F) with high humidity. Stay hydrated and limit outdoor exposure during peak heat.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Manama: +973-1724-2700 (currently operating with limited services due to security situation). Emergency: ManamaConsular@state.gov or https://mytravel.state.gov/s/crisis-intake

faq.q: Is Manama safe for tourists?
faq.a: Under normal circumstances, Manama is very safe for tourists with low crime rates and friendly locals. However, current regional tensions and armed conflict involving Iran have created elevated security risks. The US State Department advises reconsidering travel due to threats of drone and missile attacks.

faq.q: Is Manama safe at night?
faq.a: Traditionally, Manama is quite safe at night with active nightlife and good security. However, current security advisories recommend staying indoors and following shelter-in-place guidance due to ongoing regional conflict and potential attacks.

faq.q: Is Manama safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Manama is normally relatively safe for solo female travelers and is considered one of the more progressive Gulf cities. However, current security situation requires extreme caution. Women should dress modestly, stay aware of surroundings, and consider postponing travel until tensions subside.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Manama?
faq.a: Avoid older districts of Manama at night due to poor lighting. The souq area has higher petty crime rates. Currently, all non-essential movement should be avoided due to security alerts about potential missile and drone attacks.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Manama?
faq.a: Public buses and licensed taxis are generally safe and reliable. Ride-hailing apps like Uber and Careem are recommended for transparency and tracking. However, commercial flights are currently suspended, and overland routes to Saudi Arabia are the only departure option during current crisis.

cities.name: Fez
cities.summary: Fez offers a rich cultural experience but requires vigilance due to persistent hassling, pickpocketing in the medina, and common tourist scams. While violent crime is rare, the ancient winding streets create ideal conditions for petty theft and aggressive vendors targeting tourists.
cities.quickVerdict: Fez is generally safe for tourists but demands constant awareness. The medina's labyrinthine streets are hotspots for scams and pickpocketing, while female travelers should expect persistent harassment. Violent crime remains rare, and police presence is visible in tourist areas. With proper precautions and realistic expectations, visitors can safely explore Morocco's cultural capital.

neighborhoods.name: Ville Nouvelle (New City)
neighborhoods.description: Modern downtown area with wide streets, good lighting, police presence, and legitimate businesses. Most hotels, restaurants, and transport connections are here.

neighborhoods.name: Medina Bab Boujloud (Main Tourist Areas)
neighborhoods.description: Tourist-heavy areas near the main entrance with regular police patrols. Expect persistent vendors but generally manageable with firm boundaries.

neighborhoods.name: Fez El Bali Central Medina
neighborhoods.description: The ancient winding streets where most scams occur. High risk of pickpocketing, fake guides, and getting deliberately lost. Recommended to hire official guides.

neighborhoods.name: Tannery Quarter
neighborhoods.description: Notorious for aggressive touts and fake guide scams. Visitors are often led to overpriced shops or charged excessive viewing fees. Exercise extreme caution.

neighborhoods.name: Sidi Harazem Road
neighborhoods.description: Main road connecting to the new city with decent lighting and regular traffic. Generally safe for taxis and transport.

neighborhoods.name: Security Zone 3
neighborhoods.description: Area with reported increases in crime including knife-related robberies and assaults. Avoid walking alone especially after dark.

scams.name: Fake Guide Scam
scams.description: Unofficial guides approach tourists offering help or tours, leading them through confusing routes to shops for commissions or demanding large payments.
scams.howToAvoid: Only use licensed guides with official badges and ID. Book through hotels or tourist offices. Firmly decline unsolicited help.

scams.name: Tannery Viewing Scam
scams.description: Touts claim the main entrance requires high fees and offer free alternative access, then demand money once inside or lead to overpriced leather shops.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official entrances only. Research current entrance fees. Don't follow strangers claiming shops are closed or offering better deals.

scams.name: Lost Tourist Scam
scams.description: Locals deliberately give wrong directions or claim areas are closed, then offer to guide you for a fee after you become confused.
scams.howToAvoid: Download offline maps. Don't ask random people for directions. Trust your GPS over strangers claiming you're going the wrong way.

scams.name: Henna Tattoo Scam
scams.description: Women quickly apply henna to tourists' hands without permission, then demand payment for the unwanted service.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep hands away from henna artists. Say no firmly and keep walking. Don't let anyone grab your hands.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Fez requires extra vigilance due to persistent harassment and cultural attitudes. While physical safety isn't typically threatened, constant attention from men can be exhausting and intimidating. Many female travelers report feeling uncomfortable walking alone, especially in the medina.
tips: Dress conservatively with covered shoulders, arms, and legs to minimize unwanted attention
Consider hiring official guides for medina exploration to avoid harassment
Learn to say 'La' (no) firmly in Arabic and ignore catcalling completely
Stay confident and avoid looking lost or confused in public
Book accommodation in the Ville Nouvelle rather than deep in the medina

nightSafety.overview: Fez is relatively safe at night in well-lit tourist areas, but the narrow medina streets become risky after dark. Visible police presence in main areas provides some security, though response times can vary. Most locals avoid certain neighborhoods at night.
tips: Stick to the Ville Nouvelle and main tourist areas after dark
Avoid the medina's narrow alleys at night completely
Use registered taxis instead of walking for evening transportation
Stay near your accommodation after 10 PM
Travel in groups when possible during evening activities

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Fez. Local buses serve some routes but are crowded and confusing for tourists.
transport.rideshare: Careem operates in Fez and is generally reliable. Uber is not available.
transport.taxis: Petit taxis are common and affordable but insist on using the meter. Grand taxis for longer distances. Avoid unofficial taxi drivers.
transport.tips: Use registered taxis with meters, agree on prices beforehand for longer journeys, and keep small bills for exact change.

customs: Dress modestly with covered shoulders and knees, especially in religious areas
Use only your right hand for eating, greeting, and exchanging money
Remove shoes when entering homes and some traditional shops
Avoid public displays of affection and respect Islamic customs
Tip service workers appropriately (10-15% in restaurants, 5-10 dirhams for small services)

health.overview: Basic medical facilities are available in Fez, with better care in private clinics. Water quality is a concern, and food safety varies significantly between establishments.
health.water: Tap water is officially treated but not recommended for tourists. Drink bottled water and avoid ice cubes. Water pollution in local rivers poses health risks.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required beyond standard travel vaccines. Hepatitis A and B recommended. Check routine immunizations are up to date.
health.altitude: Fez is at low elevation with no altitude concerns. Mediterranean climate with hot summers and mild winters. Air pollution can affect sensitive individuals.

emergency.touristPolice: Available in tourist areas - look for Police Touristique badges
emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy in Rabat: +212-537-637-200 or US Consulate Casablanca: +212-522-264-550

faq.q: Is Fez safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Fez is generally safe with low violent crime rates, but tourists should be prepared for persistent hassling, scams, and pickpocketing in the medina. Police presence is visible in tourist areas.

faq.q: Is Fez safe at night?
faq.a: The Ville Nouvelle is relatively safe at night, but avoid the medina's narrow streets after dark. Stick to well-lit main roads and use taxis for transportation after sunset.

faq.q: Is Fez safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travel is possible but challenging due to persistent harassment. Dress conservatively, stay confident, and consider hiring official guides for medina exploration. Many women find the experience manageable but exhausting.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Fez?
faq.a: Avoid the deep medina alleys at night, the tannery quarter without official guides, and Security Zone 3. Be especially cautious around unofficial guides and anyone offering unsolicited help.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Fez?
faq.a: Registered petit taxis are generally safe and affordable. Local buses exist but can be confusing. Careem rideshare is reliable. Always insist on taxi meters and avoid unofficial drivers.

cities.name: Chefchaouen
cities.summary: Chefchaouen is widely recognized as one of Morocco's safest and most relaxed tourist destinations, known as the peaceful Blue Pearl of the Rif Mountains. The small mountain city enjoys low crime rates, friendly locals, and a strong community atmosphere that creates a secure environment for visitors.
cities.quickVerdict: Chefchaouen stands out as an exceptionally safe destination for travelers, with violent crime being virtually unheard of and petty theft being the primary but minor concern. The city's small size, visible police presence, and welcoming local community contribute to its reputation as Morocco's most peaceful tourist destination. Solo travelers, including women, consistently report feeling secure here, though basic precautions are always recommended.

neighborhoods.name: Medina (Old City)
neighborhoods.description: The historic blue-painted medina is well-patrolled by tourist police and benefits from a strong community atmosphere. Main squares like Plaza Uta el-Hammam remain lively and safe even in evenings.

neighborhoods.name: Plaza Uta el-Hammam
neighborhoods.description: The heart of Chefchaouen's old medina, this main square is consistently monitored, well-lit at night, and surrounded by cafes and restaurants. Considered the safest area in the city.

neighborhoods.name: Spanish Mosque Area
neighborhoods.description: Popular hiking destination offering panoramic views. Generally safe during daylight hours but best visited with others or as part of organized groups for sunset viewing.

neighborhoods.name: New Town (Ville Nouvelle)
neighborhoods.description: Modern part of the city where the bus station and many hotels are located. Well-developed infrastructure with good street lighting and regular police patrols.

neighborhoods.name: Bab El Ain Gate Area
neighborhoods.description: Main entrance to the medina with good foot traffic and proximity to tourist services. Taxi drop-off point for visitors, making it well-monitored and secure.

neighborhoods.name: Ras El Maa Spring
neighborhoods.description: Natural spring and waterfall area popular with tourists. Generally safe during daylight but can become isolated in evenings. Best visited in groups and avoided after dark.

scams.name: Fake Guide Scam
scams.description: Unofficial guides approach tourists offering to show secret viewpoints or best photo spots, then lead them to shops for commission or demand unexpected payment.
scams.howToAvoid: Always use licensed guides through your accommodation or tourist office. Politely decline unsolicited guide offers with a firm 'I already have a guide, thanks.'

scams.name: Overpriced Taxi Fares
scams.description: Taxi drivers may quote inflated prices to tourists, especially from the bus station to the medina or for trips to nearby attractions like Akchour Falls.
scams.howToAvoid: Know standard rates beforehand (10-20 MAD within city, 200-250 MAD round trip to Akchour). Agree on price before getting in and ask locals for current rates.

scams.name: Hashish Offers
scams.description: Local dealers frequently approach tourists offering cannabis products. While culturally accepted locally, it remains illegal and police checkpoints are common when leaving the city.
scams.howToAvoid: Firmly decline all drug offers. Possession can result in significant fines, detention, or serious legal consequences. Never buy or carry illegal substances.

scams.name: Photography Fees
scams.description: Some private courtyards and decorated areas now charge small fees (5-10 MAD) for photos, and some locals may demand payment after posing for pictures.
scams.howToAvoid: Ask about fees before taking photos, respect 'Private' signs, and always request permission before photographing people. Budget small amounts for legitimate photo spots.

soloFemale.overview: Chefchaouen is considered one of the safest destinations in Morocco for solo female travelers. The city's small size, relaxed atmosphere, and community-minded locals create a welcoming environment. Street harassment is notably less aggressive than in larger Moroccan cities, though basic cultural awareness and modest dress remain important.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees to show cultural respect and avoid unwanted attention
Stay in well-reviewed riads or hotels in the medina for authentic experience with good security
Avoid empty alleyways at night and stick to well-lit main squares like Plaza Uta el-Hammam
Use confident body language and a firm 'no thank you' if approached by persistent vendors
Connect with other travelers at popular cafes or join small group activities for added comfort

nightSafety.overview: Chefchaouen offers a magical and generally safe nighttime experience as the blue streets take on a serene glow under moonlight. The main squares and medina pathways remain active with locals and tourists in the evening. However, some precautions should be taken in quieter side streets and more isolated areas.
tips: Stick to well-lit areas like Plaza Uta el-Hammam and main medina pathways which are patrolled
Avoid dark or isolated side streets and always trust your instincts about unfamiliar areas
Travel with others when possible, especially when returning to accommodation after dinner
Keep valuables secure and maintain awareness of surroundings even in safe areas
Use hotel escort services if arriving late or unsure about navigation in the medina's winding alleys

transport.metro: No metro system exists. Chefchaouen is a small mountain city easily navigated on foot.
transport.rideshare: Rideshare apps are not available. Transport relies on traditional taxis and walking.
transport.taxis: Petit taxis (blue cars) are safe and regulated for city transport. Fares should be 10-20 MAD within the city. Grand taxis provide intercity service but agree on prices beforehand. Licensed drivers are generally trustworthy.
transport.tips: CTM buses are the safest option for intercity travel. Walking is the primary transport within the medina. Always agree on taxi fares before departure and use official taxi stands when possible.

customs: Use your right hand for eating, greetings, and exchanging money as the left hand is considered unclean in Islamic culture
Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees, especially when visiting mosques or traditional areas
Remove shoes when entering homes and some traditional establishments, following your host's lead

health.overview: Healthcare facilities in Chefchaouen are basic but adequate for minor issues. The city has local clinics and pharmacies for common problems, but serious medical emergencies may require transport to larger cities like Tangier or Casablanca.
health.water: Tap water is not recommended for drinking. Stick to bottled water which is widely available and inexpensive. Ice in tourist establishments is generally safe.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccines should be up to date. Hepatitis A recommended. Consult CDC guidelines for current recommendations. No special vaccines required for Morocco entry.
health.altitude: Chefchaouen sits at moderate elevation in the Rif Mountains. Climate is generally mild but can be cool in evenings. No altitude sickness concerns for most visitors.

emergency.touristPolice: Available in medina, ask at Plaza Uta el-Hammam
emergency.usEmbassy: US Consulate Casablanca: +212-522-64-20-00 (Emergency after hours: +212-537-63-72-00)

faq.q: Is Chefchaouen safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Chefchaouen is widely considered one of Morocco's safest cities for tourists. Violent crime is extremely rare, and the small community atmosphere creates a secure environment. Basic precautions against petty theft are sufficient.

faq.q: Is Chefchaouen safe at night?
faq.a: Chefchaouen is generally safe at night, especially in well-lit areas like Plaza Uta el-Hammam and main medina streets. However, avoid dark side alleys and isolated areas after dark, and consider traveling in groups when possible.

faq.q: Is Chefchaouen safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Chefchaouen is one of the best destinations in Morocco for solo female travelers. The city has a relaxed atmosphere with less aggressive harassment than larger cities. Modest dress and basic awareness are recommended.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Chefchaouen?
faq.a: There are no truly dangerous areas, but be cautious in isolated side streets at night, the Ras El Maa spring area after dark, and empty alleyways away from main tourist paths. Most areas are safe during daylight hours.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Chefchaouen?
faq.a: Yes, petit taxis are safe and regulated. CTM buses for intercity travel are reliable and secure. Walking is the primary way to explore the car-free medina. Always agree on taxi fares before starting your journey.

cities.name: Tangier
cities.summary: Tangier is one of Morocco's safest cities with significant security improvements in recent years and low violent crime rates. While petty theft and tourist scams occur in busy areas, the Moroccan government has invested heavily in tourism security and police presence.
cities.quickVerdict: Tangier is generally safe for tourists with violent crime being rare and strong security infrastructure. The city has transformed significantly since the 2000s with increased police presence, improved lighting, and dedicated tourism police units. Most safety concerns involve petty crime like pickpocketing in crowded areas and tourist scams in markets. Women travelers feel more comfortable here compared to other Moroccan cities like Fez or Marrakech. Basic precautions and cultural awareness ensure a trouble-free visit.

neighborhoods.name: Ville Nouvelle
neighborhoods.description: Modern commercial district with wide boulevards, hotels, and restaurants. Strong police presence and well-lit streets make it very safe for tourists day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Marshan
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential area on the hills overlooking the city. Popular with expats and tourists for its cafes, restaurants, and safer nighttime atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: Medina Old Town
neighborhoods.description: Historic medina with narrow winding streets and traditional architecture. Generally safe during daylight hours but requires extra vigilance for pickpockets and fake guides.

neighborhoods.name: Kasbah
neighborhoods.description: Ancient fortress area with museums and scenic views. Safe during day tours but avoid the outer walls at night due to poor lighting and isolation.

neighborhoods.name: Industrial Port Area
neighborhoods.description: Working port and industrial zone with limited tourist infrastructure. Higher crime rates and best avoided especially after dark unless on official business.

neighborhoods.name: Beni Makada
neighborhoods.description: Outer suburban area with socioeconomic challenges. Limited tourist facilities and higher petty crime rates make it unsuitable for visitors without local guidance.

scams.name: Fake Tour Guides
scams.description: Unofficial guides approach tourists near attractions claiming to offer cheap tours but lead to commission shops or demand large tips at the end
scams.howToAvoid: Only hire licensed guides through hotels or official agencies. Licensed guides carry official badges. Politely refuse unsolicited help.

scams.name: Overcharging in Markets
scams.description: Vendors quote inflated prices to tourists or taxi drivers refuse to use meters, especially for short trips
scams.howToAvoid: Agree on taxi fares before getting in or insist on the meter. In souks, expect to negotiate down to 50-70% of the initial asking price.

scams.name: Helpful Stranger
scams.description: Someone offers to help with directions or finding shops then demands payment for their unsolicited assistance
scams.howToAvoid: Use Google Maps for navigation. If you need help, ask shopkeepers or hotel staff rather than strangers on the street.

scams.name: Wrong Change
scams.description: Vendors or drivers give incorrect change, either less money or wrong denominations, hoping tourists won't notice
scams.howToAvoid: Learn the local currency and count change carefully. Carry small bills to avoid the no change trick. Keep large bills separate.

soloFemale.overview: Tangier is considered one of the safer Moroccan cities for solo female travelers, with many women reporting feeling more comfortable here than in Fez or Marrakech. Most safety concerns involve occasional catcalling and unwanted attention rather than serious threats. The city has strong tourism police presence and improved infrastructure.
tips: Dress modestly covering shoulders and knees to reduce unwanted attention and show cultural respect
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in safe neighborhoods like Marshan or Ville Nouvelle
Avoid walking alone in isolated areas after dark, especially around the Kasbah walls
Consider joining guided tours or making friends with other travelers for added comfort and security
Trust your instincts and enter shops or cafes if you feel uncomfortable with persistent attention

nightSafety.overview: Tangier's night safety has improved significantly with better lighting and increased police patrols. Well-lit areas like the Ville Nouvelle and parts of the medina are generally safe for evening strolls. However, avoid isolated streets and poorly lit areas, particularly around the Kasbah walls and outer neighborhoods.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and areas with good foot traffic after dark
Use official taxis or arrange transportation through your hotel for nighttime travel
Avoid the Kasbah walls and outer medina areas at night due to poor lighting
Travel in groups when possible and let someone know your whereabouts
Stay alert in tourist areas where pickpocketing can occur even in busy nightlife spots

transport.metro: Tangier has no metro system but does have local buses that connect different parts of the city. However, buses can be crowded and are not recommended for tourists unfamiliar with routes.
transport.rideshare: Uber Taxi operates connecting you with official red petit taxis through the app. InDrive is available but not officially legal. Careem operates with limited coverage mainly in Casablanca and Rabat.
transport.taxis: Red petit taxis are widely available for city travel with meters, though drivers sometimes prefer negotiated fares. White grand taxis serve intercity routes and shared rides. Always agree on fares beforehand or insist on the meter.
transport.tips: Transportation is generally safe with petit taxis being the most reliable option. The new Al-Boraq high-speed train connects to Casablanca. Always use licensed transport and avoid unmarked vehicles.

customs: Greet with As-salamu alaykum and use your right hand for eating, greeting, and exchanging items as the left hand is considered unclean
Dress modestly with covered shoulders and knees, especially in traditional areas and religious sites
Remove shoes when entering homes and mosques, and bring small gifts like pastries or tea when visiting Moroccan families
Respect Ramadan by refraining from eating, drinking, or smoking in public during daylight hours if visiting during this period
Bargaining is expected in markets but should be done respectfully, and avoid public displays of affection which are culturally inappropriate

health.overview: Tangier has adequate healthcare with private hospitals and clinics available. The city poses relatively low health risks compared to other African destinations, with no malaria risk and good medical infrastructure in urban areas.
health.water: Tap water is officially safe in Tangier due to treatment and monitoring, but travelers with sensitive stomachs should stick to bottled water. Water is suitable for showering and handwashing.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations are required for Morocco. WHO recommends routine vaccinations including Hepatitis A, and some travelers may consider Hepatitis B and typhoid depending on activities and length of stay.
health.altitude: Tangier sits at sea level on the Mediterranean coast with a temperate climate. Weather can range from mild winters to hot summers, with occasional heavy rains November through March that may cause flooding in low-lying areas.

emergency.touristPolice: Available in major tourist areas with English and Spanish speakers
emergency.usEmbassy: U.S. Consulate General Casablanca: +212 522 64 20 00. Emergency after-hours: +212 537 63 72 00. No U.S. consulate in Tangier.

faq.q: Is Tangier safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Tangier is one of Morocco's safest cities for tourists. Violent crime is rare and the government has invested heavily in tourism security. Most incidents involve petty theft or scams which can be avoided with basic precautions.

faq.q: Is Tangier safe at night?
faq.a: Tangier is generally safe at night in well-lit areas with good foot traffic. Avoid isolated streets, poorly lit areas around the Kasbah walls, and outer neighborhoods. Use official taxis for transportation after dark.

faq.q: Is Tangier safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, many solo female travelers report feeling safer in Tangier than in other Moroccan cities like Fez or Marrakech. While occasional catcalling may occur, violent crime against women is rare. Dressing modestly and staying aware of surroundings helps ensure a positive experience.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Tangier?
faq.a: Avoid the industrial port area and outer suburban neighborhoods like Beni Makada, especially after dark. The Kasbah walls area should be avoided at night due to poor lighting, though it's safe during daytime visits.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Tangier?
faq.a: Red petit taxis are safe and widely used, though always agree on fares or insist on the meter. Local buses can be crowded and confusing for tourists. The high-speed train to other cities is modern and safe. Avoid unmarked vehicles and unofficial transport.

cities.name: Essaouira
cities.summary: Essaouira is widely considered one of the safest cities in Morocco, known for its relaxed coastal atmosphere and friendly locals. The historic medina is traffic-free and petty crime is almost unheard of, making it particularly welcoming for solo travelers and families.
cities.quickVerdict: Essaouira stands out as Morocco's most laid-back and safe destination for tourists. This UNESCO World Heritage coastal city offers a refreshing contrast to busier Moroccan destinations, with fixed taxi prices, minimal harassment, and a peaceful atmosphere that appeals to solo female travelers. While standard travel precautions apply, visitors consistently report feeling secure while exploring the medina, beach, and local attractions. The city's tourism infrastructure is well-developed with dedicated tourist police presence.

neighborhoods.name: Medina (Old City)
neighborhoods.description: The UNESCO World Heritage medina is traffic-free and very safe for walking. Well-patrolled with friendly shopkeepers and artisans. Popular with tourists day and night, with clear streets and good lighting.

neighborhoods.name: Beach Area
neighborhoods.description: The main beach and waterfront promenade are safe and well-frequented. Good for morning jogs, evening walks, and water sports. Beach cafes and restaurants provide a secure environment.

neighborhoods.name: New Town (Ville Nouvelle)
neighborhoods.description: Modern commercial district with hotels, restaurants, and shops. Well-lit streets with regular police presence. Safe for evening dining and shopping.

neighborhoods.name: Port Area
neighborhoods.description: Active fishing port with colorful boats and seafood markets. Generally safe during day hours but can be busy. Evening visits should be with awareness of your surroundings.

neighborhoods.name: Mellah (Jewish Quarter)
neighborhoods.description: Historic Jewish quarter within the medina walls. Quiet residential area that's safe to explore. Some narrower alleys require standard urban awareness.

neighborhoods.name: Diabat Village
