Most hotels and lodges provide transport for evening activities, which is the safest option for tourists.
tips: Use hotel shuttles or pre-arranged taxis for evening activities rather than walking
Stay within well-lit tourist areas if you must be out after dark
Be aware that wildlife including baboons and elephants can roam into town at night
Book sunset activities through reputable operators who provide return transport
Keep emergency numbers handy and inform someone of your evening plans

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Victoria Falls. The town is small enough to walk around during daylight hours.
transport.rideshare: International rideshare apps like Uber are not available. Local taxi services and private car hire through hotels are the main options for transport.
transport.taxis: Taxis are considered safe when using reputable companies recommended by hotels. Always negotiate fares beforehand as meters are uncommon. Airport taxis are regulated and generally reliable.
transport.tips: Hotel shuttles are often the safest and most convenient transport option. For longer trips or border crossings, use established transfer companies rather than unlicensed operators.

customs: Greet people with a handshake and friendly smile - respect for elders is particularly important
Tipping is customary in restaurants (10-15%) and for guides and hotel staff, typically in USD cash
Ask permission before photographing people, especially in rural areas or markets

health.overview: Victoria Falls is in a high-risk malaria zone requiring antimalarial medication year-round, with peak transmission from November to April during the rainy season. Healthcare facilities are limited, making travel insurance with medical evacuation coverage essential.
health.water: Stick to bottled or properly treated water. Most hotels provide safe water, but avoid tap water and ice from unknown sources.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccinations plus Hepatitis A recommended for all travelers. Yellow fever vaccination required if arriving from endemic areas. Malaria prevention is crucial - consult a doctor for appropriate antimalarial medication.
health.altitude: Victoria Falls sits at low altitude (about 1000m) so altitude sickness is not a concern. The subtropical climate can be hot and humid, requiring sun protection and hydration.

emergency.touristPolice: Available in Victoria Falls town center
emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Harare: +263-867-701-1000 (2 Lorraine Drive, Bluffhill, Harare). Emergency assistance: +1-202-501-4444 (from abroad)

faq.q: Is Victoria Falls safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Victoria Falls is generally safe for tourists and is considered one of the safer destinations in Zimbabwe. The town has Tourism Police, and violent crime against tourists is rare. However, petty crime like pickpocketing occurs, so standard travel precautions are necessary.

faq.q: Is Victoria Falls safe at night?
faq.a: Victoria Falls is less safe at night, with increased risks of petty crime and wildlife encounters. Avoid walking alone after dark and use hotel transport or reputable taxis for evening activities. The main tourist areas have some lighting but caution is still advised.

faq.q: Is Victoria Falls safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Victoria Falls is relatively safe for solo female travelers and ranks as the top choice for women traveling alone in Zimbabwe. Exercise standard precautions, choose secure accommodation, avoid walking alone at night, and be prepared for persistent but generally harmless vendor approaches.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Victoria Falls?
faq.a: Avoid outlying areas after dark, be cautious in Chinotimba township without a guide, and stay away from the river edge outside designated areas. The main tourist zones are generally safe, but always remain alert for petty crime.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Victoria Falls?
faq.a: Public transport options are limited but generally safe during daylight. Hotel shuttles are the safest option, followed by reputable taxi services recommended by accommodation. Avoid unlicensed operators and negotiate fares beforehand.

cities.name: Windhoek
cities.summary: Windhoek, Namibia's capital, is moderately safe for tourists but requires vigilance, particularly after dark. The city faces challenges with petty crime, vehicle break-ins, and occasional violent incidents, though natural disasters pose minimal risk.
cities.quickVerdict: Windhoek offers a fascinating cultural experience but demands cautious travel planning. While generally safer than many African capitals during daytime, crime rates are elevated with particular risks around theft, carjacking, and muggings. The city ranks 7th highest in Africa for crime index but remains accessible to prepared travelers who take appropriate precautions. Infrastructure is adequate with good private healthcare options, though public transport is limited and not always reliable for tourists.

neighborhoods.name: Klein Windhoek
neighborhoods.description: Affluent residential area popular with expats and tourists. Well-maintained streets, good restaurants, and generally secure environment during day and early evening hours.

neighborhoods.name: City Centre/CBD
neighborhoods.description: Commercial heart with shops, restaurants, and historic sites like Christuskirche. Generally safe during business hours but requires increased vigilance, especially around Independence Avenue.

neighborhoods.name: Windhoek West
neighborhoods.description: Mixed residential and commercial area with shopping centers. Reasonable safety during daytime but avoid walking alone after dark, particularly in less populated sections.

neighborhoods.name: Eros
neighborhoods.description: Upmarket suburb near the airport with good accommodation options. Generally secure with lower crime rates, though standard precautions still advised for visitors.

neighborhoods.name: Katutura
neighborhoods.description: Historic township with vibrant culture but elevated crime risks. While culturally significant, solo visitors should join guided tours and avoid independent exploration, especially at night.

neighborhoods.name: Khomasdal
neighborhoods.description: Large residential area with mixed safety conditions. Some sections relatively safe during day, but crime risks increase significantly after sunset. Local knowledge essential.

scams.name: Palm Nut Carving Scam
scams.description: Friendly locals approach tourists asking for their name, carve it into a palm nut, then demand payment of $70 or more for the 'custom' souvenir.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline any offers to carve your name or create personalized items. Walk away immediately if someone starts carving without your explicit agreement.

scams.name: Car Distraction Theft
scams.description: One person distracts you while parking or at traffic lights (asking directions, pointing out car problems) while accomplices steal belongings from your vehicle.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep car doors locked at all times, windows up in traffic, and valuables out of sight. Don't engage with strangers who approach your vehicle.

scams.name: Overpriced Taxi Rides
scams.description: Taxi drivers charge inflated fares to tourists, especially from the airport, or claim meters are broken to justify excessive charges.
scams.howToAvoid: Always agree on fare before starting journey, use reputable taxi companies like City Cab, or book airport transfers in advance through hotels.

scams.name: Fake Tourist Police
scams.description: Scammers pose as tourist police demanding to search bags or check identification, then steal valuables or demand bribes.
scams.howToAvoid: Namibia has no tourist police division. Ask for proper identification, request to go to the nearest police station, and never hand over documents or money on the street.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Windhoek requires heightened awareness and careful planning. While many women travel here successfully, the city presents elevated risks including harassment in busy areas and safety concerns after dark. Accommodation in upmarket areas like Klein Windhoek is recommended, and joining group tours provides added security.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in safer neighborhoods like Klein Windhoek or Eros
Avoid walking alone after dark anywhere in the city - use reputable taxi services instead
Dress modestly to minimize unwanted attention, covering shoulders and legs in public areas
Join organized tours rather than exploring independently, especially for township visits
Keep regular contact with family/friends and share your itinerary with trusted contacts

nightSafety.overview: Windhoek's safety deteriorates significantly after dark, with increased risks of violent crime, muggings, and carjackings. Most tourist areas become unsafe for pedestrians at night. The nightlife scene exists mainly in central areas and specific venues, but getting to and from locations safely requires careful planning.
tips: Never walk alone at night - use reputable taxis or ride services for all nighttime transportation
Stick to well-known venues in central areas and avoid wandering between locations on foot
Travel in groups when possible and pre-arrange safe transportation back to accommodation
Avoid displaying expensive items or large amounts of cash during nighttime activities
Stay alert for groups of young men who may be looking for trouble, particularly in township areas

transport.metro: Windhoek has no metro system. Public transport consists mainly of minibus taxis that can be overcrowded and confusing for tourists, with limited route information available.
transport.rideshare: Yango operates in Windhoek offering more affordable and safer alternatives to traditional taxis. LEFA is another app-based service popular with locals and visitors.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxi companies like Windhoek City Cab and Dial-a-Cab offer reliable service. Avoid hailing taxis on the street - book through hotels or call companies directly. Expect to pay NAD 60-80 for trips within the city center.
transport.tips: Pre-book airport transfers through reputable companies. Always confirm taxi driver identity and agree on fares beforehand. Keep car doors locked when driving and avoid stopping for strangers flagging you down.

customs: Greet people warmly with handshakes and pleasantries before conducting business - rushing into conversations is considered rude
Show respect for elders and traditional authority figures, removing hats when entering homes and following host protocols
Always ask permission before photographing people, especially in rural areas or townships where some may expect small donations
Dress modestly when visiting villages or religious sites, with covered shoulders and legs showing cultural sensitivity
Learn basic greetings in local languages like 'Wa lalapo' (Oshiwambo) or 'Koës' (Nama) to show respect for cultural diversity

health.overview: Windhoek has adequate healthcare with several private hospitals offering international-standard care, though public facilities face resource constraints. Private medical insurance is strongly recommended as quality care can be expensive.
health.water: Municipal water supply is generally safe to drink in Windhoek, though bottled water is advisable for sensitive stomachs. Avoid drinking from rivers or untreated sources outside the city.
health.vaccinations: Yellow fever vaccination required if arriving from endemic areas. Routine vaccines (MMR, DPT, flu) should be up to date. Malaria risk exists in northern regions but not in Windhoek itself.
health.altitude: Windhoek sits at 1,600m elevation which may cause mild altitude effects for some visitors. The climate is hot and dry with intense UV radiation requiring sun protection and adequate hydration.

emergency.touristPolice: N/A - No tourist police division exists
emergency.usEmbassy: 38 Metje Street, Klein Windhoek. Phone: +264 61 202 5000. Emergency after-hours: +264 61 202 5000

faq.q: Is Windhoek safe for tourists?
faq.a: Windhoek is moderately safe for tourists who take appropriate precautions. While crime rates are elevated compared to Western standards, thousands visit safely each year. Key precautions include avoiding walking at night, using reputable transportation, and staying in secure accommodations.

faq.q: Is Windhoek safe at night?
faq.a: Windhoek becomes significantly less safe after dark with increased risks of violent crime and muggings. Walking at night is strongly discouraged anywhere in the city. Use reputable taxis or pre-arranged transportation for all nighttime travel.

faq.q: Is Windhoek safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travelers can visit Windhoek safely with careful planning and heightened awareness. Choose accommodations in upmarket areas, join organized tours instead of exploring independently, dress modestly, and maintain regular contact with family or friends about your whereabouts.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Windhoek?
faq.a: Exercise extreme caution in Katutura township, especially at night, despite its cultural significance. Avoid remote areas of Khomasdal after dark and be vigilant in any area outside the city center once the sun sets. Even safe areas require precautions after dark.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Windhoek?
faq.a: Public minibus taxis are generally safe during daytime but can be overcrowded and confusing for tourists. For greater safety and convenience, use reputable taxi companies like Windhoek City Cab, or app-based services like Yango and LEFA rather than public transport.

cities.name: Swakopmund
cities.summary: Swakopmund is generally considered one of Namibia's safest tourist destinations, benefiting from a dedicated Tourist Protection Unit and relatively low violent crime rates. While petty crime such as pickpocketing and theft from vehicles occurs, particularly around tourist areas and ATMs, serious incidents targeting foreigners are rare.
cities.quickVerdict: Swakopmund offers a safe environment for tourists with proper precautions. The coastal town has lower crime rates than Windhoek and benefits from good police presence including tourist-specific units. Most visitors can explore comfortably during the day, though standard urban safety measures apply, especially after dark. The town's compact size, tourist infrastructure, and community-oriented culture contribute to overall safety.

neighborhoods.name: Swakopmund Central
neighborhoods.description: The historic city center with German colonial architecture, museums, and main tourist attractions. Well-patrolled and generally safe during day and evening hours with good lighting and foot traffic.

neighborhoods.name: Tamariskia
neighborhoods.description: Upmarket residential area popular with tourists, featuring guesthouses and holiday accommodations. Quiet neighborhood with low crime rates and good security measures.

neighborhoods.name: Vineta
neighborhoods.description: Mixed residential and commercial area with shops and restaurants. Generally safe but requires normal precautions, especially when walking alone after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Kramersdorf
neighborhoods.description: Primarily residential area with some tourist accommodations. Safe during daylight but limited lighting at night requires extra caution.

neighborhoods.name: Mondesa
neighborhoods.description: Township area with higher crime statistics including theft and assault cases. Visitors should exercise increased vigilance and avoid walking alone, particularly after sunset.

neighborhoods.name: Industrial Areas
neighborhoods.description: Commercial and industrial zones on the outskirts of town. Higher risk of crime with limited security and poor lighting. Not recommended for tourists, especially after dark.

scams.name: Fake Camera Damage
scams.description: Locals ask tourists to take their photo, then deliberately drop the camera and blame the tourist for breaking it, demanding compensation.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline to handle others' cameras or electronic devices. If you do help, ensure multiple witnesses are present.

scams.name: Bar Overcharging Scam
scams.description: Friendly locals invite tourists to specific bars where drinks are severely overpriced. Bills can be 5-10 times normal rates with intimidation if payment is refused.
scams.howToAvoid: Choose bars yourself, always check menu prices before ordering, and be wary of overly friendly strangers suggesting specific venues.

scams.name: ATM Helper Scam
scams.description: Someone approaches offering help with ATM transactions to avoid fees, but uses hidden card skimmers to steal card details and PIN numbers.
scams.howToAvoid: Never accept help at ATMs, cover your PIN entry, use machines inside banks when possible, and report suspicious devices immediately.

scams.name: Overpriced Tour Packages
scams.description: Unofficial operators offer safari tours and desert activities at inflated prices, often with substandard service or safety standards.
scams.howToAvoid: Book through reputable, licensed tour operators, compare prices from multiple sources, and verify credentials with your accommodation.

soloFemale.overview: Swakopmund is generally safe for solo female travelers with appropriate precautions. The town has relatively low crime rates and a tourist-friendly atmosphere. However, women should remain vigilant, especially at night, and take standard safety measures to avoid unwanted attention or petty crime.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in central areas like Tamariskia or city center
Avoid walking alone after dark, use taxis or rideshare services for evening activities
Dress modestly, especially when exploring beyond tourist areas
Keep valuables secure and avoid displaying expensive items like jewelry or cameras
Join group tours for desert activities rather than venturing alone into remote areas

nightSafety.overview: Swakopmund's nighttime safety is moderate with some areas safer than others. The city center remains relatively active in the evening with restaurants and bars, but petty crime increases after dark. Most incidents involve theft from vehicles or pickpocketing rather than violent crime.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and avoid isolated areas, especially near the harbor
Travel in groups when possible and use taxis for longer distances
Keep car doors locked and windows up, don't leave valuables visible in vehicles
Be extra cautious around ATMs and avoid withdrawing cash late at night
Stay alert in bars and nightlife areas where pickpocketing is more common

transport.metro: Swakopmund has no metro system. Public transport is limited to minibus taxis and intercity buses.
transport.rideshare: No formal rideshare apps operate in Swakopmund. Use hotel-arranged taxis or call reputable taxi companies directly.
transport.taxis: Taxis are available but quality varies significantly. Some drivers have been involved in robberies. Use only recommended services arranged through hotels or Tourist Protection Units. Agree on fares beforehand as meters are rare.
transport.tips: Rental cars are the preferred transport method for tourists. Book through established companies and ensure comprehensive insurance. Avoid hitchhiking or accepting rides from strangers, as several tourist robberies have occurred this way.

customs: Extended greetings and firm handshakes are very important - always greet people before asking for help or conducting business
Show respect by touching your right elbow with your left hand while shaking hands, especially with elders
Accept offered food and drinks as refusal can be seen as impolite in Namibian culture
Dress modestly, especially in rural areas - avoid revealing clothing and public displays of affection
Always ask permission before photographing people, and expect to pay a small fee in traditional communities

health.overview: Swakopmund has good private healthcare facilities with English-speaking doctors. The dry coastal climate reduces disease risks compared to northern Namibia. Medical evacuation insurance is strongly recommended for serious emergencies.
health.water: Tap water in Swakopmund is generally safe to drink as it meets municipal standards. However, bottled water is readily available and recommended for sensitive stomachs.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccines are recommended including Hepatitis A, Hepatitis B, and Typhoid. Yellow fever vaccination is only required if arriving from endemic countries. Malaria risk is very low in coastal areas.
health.altitude: Swakopmund sits at sea level with a desert climate. Sun protection is essential due to intense UV levels. Temperature variations between day and night can be significant.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Windhoek: +(264)(61) 202-5000, 38 Metje Street, Klein Windhoek. Emergency After-Hours: +(264)(61) 202-5000

faq.q: Is Swakopmund safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Swakopmund is generally safe for tourists and is considered one of Namibia's safest destinations. It has dedicated Tourist Protection Units and lower crime rates than larger cities like Windhoek. However, visitors should still take standard precautions against petty crime.

faq.q: Is Swakopmund safe at night?
faq.a: Swakopmund is moderately safe at night, especially in the central tourist areas. However, petty crime increases after dark, so avoid walking alone, stick to well-lit areas, and use taxis for transportation. The main risks are pickpocketing and theft from vehicles.

faq.q: Is Swakopmund safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Swakopmund is generally safe for solo female travelers who take appropriate precautions. Stay in central accommodations, avoid walking alone at night, dress modestly, and use reputable tour operators. The town has a tourist-friendly atmosphere with good support infrastructure.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Swakopmund?
faq.a: Avoid the Mondesa township area, especially after dark, where crime statistics are higher. Industrial areas on the outskirts also pose increased risks. Stay away from isolated beaches at night and be cautious around the harbor area after sunset.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Swakopmund?
faq.a: Public transport options are limited and quality varies. Some taxi drivers have been involved in robberies, so only use recommended services arranged through hotels. Rental cars are the preferred option for tourists, offering greater safety and convenience.

cities.name: Gaborone
cities.summary: Gaborone, Botswana's capital, is generally considered one of Africa's safer cities with a stable political environment and moderate crime rates. While violent crime against tourists is relatively rare, petty theft, pickpocketing, and opportunistic crimes do occur, particularly in crowded areas and at night. The city offers a good foundation for safe travel with proper precautions.
cities.quickVerdict: Gaborone presents a moderately safe environment for tourists, ranking as one of the safer African capitals. The US State Department maintains a Level 2 advisory for Botswana, citing crime as the primary concern. While serious violent crime against visitors is uncommon, property crimes like theft, burglary, and car break-ins occur regularly. The city's infrastructure is well-developed with reliable emergency services, and locals are generally friendly and helpful. Solo female travelers can visit safely with standard precautions, though extra caution is advised at night and in certain areas like Kgale Hill and Gaborone Dam.

neighborhoods.name: CBD (Central Business District)
neighborhoods.description: The city center is well-patrolled and generally safe during business hours with good lighting and security presence. Popular for shopping and dining but requires caution after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Phakalane
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential area with golf courses and good security infrastructure. Well-planned suburb with minimal crime reports and family-friendly atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: Village/Sebele
neighborhoods.description: Mixed residential area with varying safety levels. Some parts are perfectly safe while others require extra vigilance, especially regarding petty theft.

neighborhoods.name: Gaborone Dam Area
neighborhoods.description: Popular recreational area but known for muggings and robberies, particularly during evening hours. Avoid visiting alone and stick to daylight hours.

neighborhoods.name: Kgale Hill
neighborhoods.description: Hiking spot with repeated reports of muggings and armed robberies. Only visit with organized groups or local guides, never alone.

neighborhoods.name: Block Areas (Various)
neighborhoods.description: Residential areas with mixed safety records. Some blocks are safer than others, but general caution is advised, especially regarding home invasions and burglaries.

scams.name: Fake Camera Damage
scams.description: Locals ask tourists to take their photo, then deliberately drop the camera claiming it's broken and demanding payment for repairs.
scams.howToAvoid: Politely decline photo requests from strangers. If you agree to help, examine the camera first and be firm about not paying for pre-existing damage.

scams.name: Bar/Club Overcharging
scams.description: Friendly locals invite tourists to bars where drinks are severely overpriced (5-10x normal rates), with intimidating staff demanding payment.
scams.howToAvoid: Suggest your own venue choice, always check menu prices before ordering, and be prepared to leave if prices seem excessive.

scams.name: Fake Police/Official Scam
scams.description: Criminals pose as tourist police or officials demanding document checks, fines, or bribes. Note: There is no official 'tourist police' division in Botswana.
scams.howToAvoid: Always ask for proper identification and verify with local police stations. Real police don't demand immediate cash payments for violations.

scams.name: ATM Assistance Fraud
scams.description: Scammers offer to help with ATM transactions, claiming to avoid bank fees, while secretly trying to observe PIN numbers or clone cards.
scams.howToAvoid: Never accept help at ATMs, shield your PIN, and use machines at banks with security guards when possible. Report suspicious activity immediately.

soloFemale.overview: Gaborone is relatively safe for solo female travelers, ranking among the better options in Africa. The local culture generally respects women, and incidents of harassment are less common than in many regional destinations. However, standard precautions are essential, particularly regarding nighttime activities and isolated areas. The city has adequate infrastructure and emergency services to support solo travelers.
tips: Avoid walking alone at night, especially in poorly lit areas and around Gaborone Dam or Kgale Hill
Dress modestly, particularly when visiting rural areas or traditional sites - cover shoulders and avoid revealing clothing
Use reputable taxis or arrange transportation through your hotel rather than walking alone after dark
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in safer neighborhoods like Phakalane or the CBD area
Be aware that curious locals may ask about your marital status - wearing a wedding ring can deflect unwanted attention

nightSafety.overview: Nighttime safety in Gaborone requires extra caution, with increased risks of petty crime, muggings, and opportunistic theft after dark. While the city center and popular areas like Masa Square have better lighting and some security presence, many residential areas become less safe. The nightlife scene is active but concentrated in specific areas, and using reliable transportation is essential.
tips: Always use licensed taxis (blue plates) or arranged transport - avoid walking between venues at night
Stay in groups when out nightlife hopping and stick to well-established venues in CBD or Masa Square area
Be extremely cautious around Gaborone Dam and Kgale Hill areas during evening hours - these are known crime hotspots
Keep valuables secure and avoid displaying expensive items, phones, or large amounts of cash
Plan your route back to accommodation in advance and don't rely on finding transport late at night

transport.metro: No metro system exists in Gaborone.
transport.rideshare: Limited rideshare options with some apps like iDrive available, but reliability can be inconsistent. Most visitors report mixed success with app-based services.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis with blue plates are generally safe and reliable. Always agree on fare before departure as meters aren't always used. Hotel/restaurant bookings recommended for evening transport.
transport.tips: Public combis (minibuses) are affordable but can be overcrowded and poorly maintained - not recommended for tourists. Licensed taxi services like Gifa's Transport receive excellent reviews for reliability and safety.

customs: Greetings are very important - always acknowledge people with a handshake using the right hand, and show extra respect to elders by using both hands or slight bowing
The concept of 'Botho' (Ubuntu) emphasizes respect, kindness, and compassion - be patient, polite, and avoid confrontational behavior
Remove hats when greeting others, especially elders or in formal settings, and avoid pointing at people with your finger - use your whole hand instead

health.overview: Healthcare in Gaborone is adequate for basic needs with both public and private facilities available. Princess Marina Hospital is the main public facility, while private clinics offer higher-standard care.
health.water: Tap water in Gaborone is generally safe to drink as it meets international standards, but bottled water is recommended for sensitive stomachs.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Gaborone itself. Yellow fever certificate needed if arriving from endemic areas. Standard travel vaccines (hepatitis A/B, typhoid) recommended.
health.altitude: Gaborone sits at 1,014 meters (3,327 feet) above sea level - no altitude concerns. Climate is semi-arid with hot summers and mild winters. Take precautions against strong sun exposure.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy: Embassy Drive, Government Enclave, Gaborone. Phone: +267 395-3982. Emergency after hours: +267 373-2222

faq.q: Is Gaborone safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Gaborone is generally safe for tourists and considered one of Africa's safer capitals. While petty crime occurs, violent crime against tourists is relatively rare. Standard travel precautions are sufficient for most visitors.

faq.q: Is Gaborone safe at night?
faq.a: Nighttime safety requires extra caution. Avoid walking alone after dark, use licensed taxis, and stay in well-lit, populated areas. Certain areas like Gaborone Dam and Kgale Hill should be avoided entirely at night.

faq.q: Is Gaborone safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, with proper precautions. Gaborone is relatively safe for solo women compared to regional standards. Dress modestly, avoid isolated areas at night, and use reputable accommodation and transport.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Gaborone?
faq.a: Exercise caution around Gaborone Dam and avoid Kgale Hill unless with organized groups. Some residential 'block' areas require vigilance, and any isolated areas should be avoided after dark.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Gaborone?
faq.a: Licensed taxis (blue plates) are safe and recommended. Public combis (minibuses) are used by locals but can be overcrowded and poorly maintained - better to use taxis or arranged transport for tourists.

cities.name: Maputo
cities.summary: Maputo presents moderate safety risks typical of a major African capital city. While violent crime exists, most visitors encounter petty theft and scams rather than serious safety issues. Recent improvements in political stability have enhanced the overall security environment.
cities.quickVerdict: Maputo requires heightened caution but is generally safer than many neighboring capitals like Johannesburg. Petty crime is common, especially in crowded areas and markets. Violent crime occurs but often targets locals rather than tourists. Night activities should be avoided, particularly walking alone. With proper precautions - staying in safer neighborhoods, using reliable transport, and remaining vigilant - most visitors have trouble-free stays.

neighborhoods.name: Polana Cimento
neighborhoods.description: Upscale diplomatic and business district with embassies, luxury hotels, and good security. Well-lit streets with regular police patrols make it the safest area for tourists.

neighborhoods.name: Sommerschield
neighborhoods.description: Modern residential area popular with expats, featuring shopping centers and restaurants. Good infrastructure and security presence make it relatively safe day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Baixa (Downtown)
neighborhoods.description: Historic city center with colonial architecture and markets. Safe during daylight hours but requires caution due to pickpocketing and crowded conditions.

neighborhoods.name: Costa do Sol
neighborhoods.description: Coastal area known for beaches and nightlife. Generally safe during day but has official beach curfew from 7 PM to 5 AM due to increased crime incidents.

neighborhoods.name: Xipamanine
neighborhoods.description: Dense township area with high poverty levels. Significantly higher crime rates and not recommended for tourists without local guides.

neighborhoods.name: Mafalala
neighborhoods.description: Informal settlement with limited infrastructure and high crime rates. Known for muggings and theft, particularly dangerous for foreign visitors.

scams.name: Overcharging by Taxi Drivers
scams.description: Unofficial taxi drivers quote inflated prices to tourists, especially at the airport and popular destinations.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official taxi apps like Yango or Viva Taxi, agree on prices beforehand, or ask locals about standard fares.

scams.name: Fake Tour Guides
scams.description: Individuals approach tourists offering unofficial guide services at inflated prices or lead them to unsafe areas.
scams.howToAvoid: Only use reputable tour companies, verify credentials, and book through hotels or official tourism offices.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Scams
scams.description: Street money changers offer better rates but provide counterfeit bills or shortchange tourists during the exchange.
scams.howToAvoid: Use official banks or exchange bureaus, avoid street exchanges, and verify bills immediately.

scams.name: Police Corruption
scams.description: Some police officers may solicit bribes from tourists for minor infractions or during document checks.
scams.howToAvoid: Carry proper documentation, remain polite but firm, ask for official receipts, and report incidents to your embassy.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Maputo requires extra caution due to increased harassment risks and cultural factors. While many women travel safely, incidents of unwanted attention, sexual harassment, and assault have been reported. Local authorities advise female visitors to travel with companions whenever possible.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in safer neighborhoods like Polana Cimento or Sommerschield
Avoid walking alone after dark and use reliable taxi services for evening transport
Dress modestly to respect local culture and reduce unwanted attention
Keep emergency contacts readily available and inform someone of your daily plans
Trust your instincts and avoid isolated areas, particularly beaches and markets during quiet hours

nightSafety.overview: Maputo becomes significantly less safe after dark, with increased criminal activity including armed robbery, muggings, and sexual assaults. The US State Department advises avoiding all non-emergency travel between sunset and sunrise. Even popular tourist areas see heightened crime at night.
tips: Return to your accommodation before sunset and avoid walking outdoors at night
Use only reputable taxi services or ride-sharing apps for evening transportation
Stay in areas with active nightlife and good lighting if you must go out
Inform others of your plans and expected return time when going out after dark
Be aware that Costa do Sol beach has an official curfew from 7 PM to 5 AM due to crime

transport.metro: Maputo does not have a metro system. Public buses (machinabombo) exist but are unreliable, overcrowded, and not recommended for tourists due to safety concerns.
transport.rideshare: Ride-sharing apps are available including Yango (cheapest), Viva Taxi (mid-range safety), and Taxi Marcello (safest but most expensive). Apps require local SIM cards and basic Portuguese. Most drivers don't speak English.
transport.taxis: Official yellow-green taxis are available but don't use meters, requiring price negotiation. Costs typically range from 200-800 MZN depending on distance. Use taxis recommended by hotels when possible.
transport.tips: Chapas (minibuses) are very cheap but extremely overcrowded and potentially unsafe. Avoid 'My Loves' (open-back trucks) which are dangerous. For night travel, use only the most reputable taxi services.

customs: Greet people with handshakes and eye contact, using formal titles like Senhor/Senhora initially
Remove shoes before entering homes and accept offered tea or snacks when visiting locals
Dress modestly with covered thighs for women and long pants for men, especially in rural areas
Ask permission before photographing people and avoid photos of government buildings
Use your right hand for eating and passing items as it's considered more respectful

health.overview: Maputo faces significant health challenges with weak healthcare infrastructure and limited medical supplies. Private facilities offer better care but require cash payments upfront.
health.water: Tap water is not safe to drink. Use bottled water for drinking, brushing teeth, and ice cubes. Boiling or chemical disinfection is necessary for tap water use.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccines should be current. Hepatitis A, typhoid, and yellow fever (if arriving from endemic areas) are recommended. MMR vaccination is important due to ongoing global measles outbreaks.
health.altitude: Maputo is at sea level. Climate is tropical with high temperatures year-round. Malaria is a serious risk requiring prophylaxis and prevention measures including insect repellent and bed nets.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Maputo: +258 21 492797. Located at Avenida Kenneth Kaunda 193, Maputo. For after-hours emergencies: +258 84 304 4600

faq.q: Is Maputo safe for tourists?
faq.a: Maputo is moderately safe for tourists who take proper precautions. While violent crime exists, most visitors experience only petty theft. Stay in safer neighborhoods, use reliable transport, avoid night activities, and remain vigilant in crowded areas.

faq.q: Is Maputo safe at night?
faq.a: No, Maputo becomes significantly more dangerous after dark. The US State Department advises avoiding all non-emergency travel between sunset and sunrise. Criminal activity including armed robbery and muggings increases substantially at night.

faq.q: Is Maputo safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travel in Maputo requires extra caution. While possible, women face increased risks of harassment and assault. Authorities recommend traveling with companions, staying in safer areas, dressing modestly, and avoiding isolated locations.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Maputo?
faq.a: Avoid Mafalala and Xipamanine neighborhoods due to high crime rates. Stay away from Avenida Marginal between Southern Sun Hotel and Waterfront Restaurant. Avoid all beaches after 7 PM due to official curfews, and don't venture into informal settlements without guides.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Maputo?
faq.a: Public buses and chapas are not recommended for tourists due to overcrowding and safety concerns. Use ride-sharing apps like Yango, Viva Taxi, or official taxis instead. For night travel, use only the most reputable taxi services like Taxi Marcello.

cities.name: Dakar
cities.summary: Dakar is generally safe for tourists with a moderate crime index, but travelers should remain vigilant against petty crimes, scams, and street harassment. The city offers good healthcare facilities and efficient transport options but requires standard urban safety precautions.
cities.quickVerdict: Dakar presents a reasonably safe environment for tourists who take proper precautions. While violent crime rates are relatively low, petty theft, pickpocketing, and tourist scams are common, particularly in busy markets and tourist areas. Solo female travelers may experience harassment but generally report feeling safe during daytime. The city benefits from decent infrastructure, reliable taxis, and adequate medical facilities. Most visitors have positive experiences by staying alert, avoiding certain neighborhoods at night, and following local customs.

neighborhoods.name: Les Almadies
neighborhoods.description: Upscale coastal district with luxury hotels, fine dining, and diplomatic residences. Well-patrolled and considered the safest area for tourists with excellent amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Plateau
neighborhoods.description: Downtown business district with government buildings and major attractions. Generally safe during daytime but can have petty crime issues, especially around Place de l'Independence.

neighborhoods.name: Medina
neighborhoods.description: Historic traditional quarter with local markets and authentic culture. Higher risk of pickpocketing and scams targeting tourists, requires extra vigilance.

neighborhoods.name: Corniche Ouest
neighborhoods.description: Scenic coastal road popular with tourists but known for regular robberies. Exercise caution especially when walking alone or after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Sandaga Market Area
neighborhoods.description: Busy commercial district with intense market activity. High risk of pickpocketing, aggressive vendors, and tourist scams. Avoid carrying valuables.

neighborhoods.name: Pikine
neighborhoods.description: Densely populated suburb with higher crime rates. Not recommended for tourists, especially at night. Locals advise avoiding this area entirely.

scams.name: Fake Gold Bracelet
scams.description: Scammers approach tourists claiming to have just had a baby and offer 'gold' bracelets as gifts, then demand money for baby milk.
scams.howToAvoid: Firmly decline any unsolicited gifts and walk away. Don't engage in conversation or accept items from strangers.

scams.name: Airport Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Unofficial taxi drivers at Blaise Diagne Airport charge inflated fares, sometimes 3-4 times the normal rate to disoriented tourists.
scams.howToAvoid: Pre-book transport online, use official taxi companies, or know the standard fare is around 12,000-20,000 CFA to central Dakar.

scams.name: Currency Exchange Fraud
scams.description: Street money changers offer better rates but provide counterfeit bills or short-change tourists during the transaction.
scams.howToAvoid: Only exchange money at banks, official exchange bureaus, or reliable ATMs. Avoid street-side currency exchanges.

scams.name: Romance Scams
scams.description: Online or in-person romantic relationships developed to eventually request money for emergencies or travel expenses.
scams.howToAvoid: Be cautious of new romantic interests asking for money. Verify identities and be skeptical of sob stories requiring financial help.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travel in Dakar is generally manageable but requires extra caution. Women report persistent male attention and harassment, especially in markets and busy streets. While physical safety is usually not a concern during daytime, the emotional toll of constant vigilance can impact the travel experience. Many female travelers recommend joining group activities or hiring local guides for added security.
tips: Dress modestly and respect local Islamic customs, especially during Ramadan
Avoid walking alone after dark and stick to well-lit, populated areas
Firmly say 'Jerejef' (no thank you in Wolof) to persistent vendors or unwanted attention
Keep valuables in a zipped bag worn across your chest rather than in open pockets
Consider staying in upscale neighborhoods like Les Almadies for added security

nightSafety.overview: Dakar's nighttime safety is moderate, with about 59% of residents and visitors feeling secure during evening hours. Tourist areas generally maintain reasonable safety levels after dark, but petty crime risks increase. Well-lit areas and popular venues are safer options for evening activities.
tips: Stay in groups when going out at night and avoid isolated areas
Use registered yellow and black taxis rather than walking long distances
Stick to well-established restaurants and venues in safe neighborhoods
Keep emergency numbers handy and inform someone of your evening plans
Avoid displaying expensive items or large amounts of cash after dark

transport.metro: Dakar has modern transport infrastructure including the BRT system launched in 2024 and the TER regional train connecting downtown to the airport. Public buses (Dakar Dem Dikk) are considered safe but can have long wait times and limited seating.
transport.rideshare: Yango is the primary ride-hailing app available in Dakar. The service is reliable and useful for tourists, offering transparent pricing and tracking features. Pre-booking through apps is recommended for airport transfers.
transport.taxis: Yellow and black registered taxis are the safest transport option. Most don't use meters, so negotiate fares in advance - expect around 300 CFA per kilometer. Avoid motorbike taxis and informal 'car rapides' as advised by the US State Department.
transport.tips: Pre-book airport transfers to avoid overcharging scams. Always use official taxis and confirm fares before starting your journey. The BRT and TER systems are modern and safe for tourists.

customs: Greet people with extended handshakes while inquiring about family health and welfare - this is fundamental to Senegalese social interaction
Respect the concept of 'Teranga' (hospitality) by graciously accepting offered tea or refreshments, as refusing may be seen as rejection
Show deep respect for elders by greeting them first when entering rooms and using formal titles like 'papa' or 'mama' even for non-relatives
Use your right hand for eating, greeting, and gift-giving - never use the left hand which is considered unclean
Remove shoes before entering someone's home and wash hands before meals as cleanliness is very important in Islamic culture

health.overview: Healthcare in Dakar is adequate with several hospitals and clinics capable of handling major injuries and illnesses. However, facilities outside Dakar are extremely limited. The current Rift Valley Fever outbreak requires extra precautions against mosquito bites.
health.water: Drink only bottled or properly filtered water to avoid waterborne illnesses. Tap water is not considered safe for tourists to consume.
health.vaccinations: Ensure routine vaccinations are current including MMR and polio. Malaria prophylaxis is essential. Check CDC recommendations for yellow fever and other region-specific vaccines.
health.altitude: Dakar is at sea level with a tropical climate. The Harmattan winds from November to April can cause respiratory issues and reduced visibility due to Saharan dust.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Dakar: +221 33 879 4000 (24/7 emergency line). Located at Route des Almadies, Dakar. For US citizens abroad: +1-202-501-4444

faq.q: Is Dakar safe for tourists?
faq.a: Dakar is moderately safe for tourists who take proper precautions. While violent crime is relatively rare, petty theft and scams are common in tourist areas. Stay alert, don't display valuables, and avoid isolated areas especially at night.

faq.q: Is Dakar safe at night?
faq.a: Nighttime safety in Dakar is moderate. About 59% of visitors feel secure after dark in tourist areas. Stick to well-lit, populated areas, travel in groups, and use registered taxis rather than walking long distances.

faq.q: Is Dakar safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travel is possible but requires extra caution. Expect persistent attention and harassment, especially in markets. Dress modestly, avoid walking alone after dark, and consider staying in upscale areas like Les Almadies for added security.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Dakar?
faq.a: Avoid Pikine and Guédiawaye suburbs, especially at night. Be cautious around Sandaga Market, Corniche Ouest after dark, and the airport area due to scam risks. Stick to safer neighborhoods like Les Almadies and central Plateau during the day.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Dakar?
faq.a: Public transport is generally safe. The new BRT system and TER train are modern and secure. Yellow registered taxis are the safest option - negotiate fares in advance. Avoid motorbike taxis and informal 'car rapides' as advised by authorities.

cities.name: Abidjan
cities.summary: Abidjan is West Africa's economic hub with a vibrant cultural scene, but visitors need to exercise caution due to moderate crime rates and security challenges. The city offers rich experiences for prepared travelers who stay alert and follow safety guidelines.
cities.quickVerdict: Abidjan presents a mixed safety picture for tourists. While violent crime against foreigners is not extremely common, petty crime including theft, pickpocketing, and scams occurs regularly, particularly in crowded areas and tourist zones. The US State Department maintains a Level 2 advisory for Ivory Coast, citing concerns about crime, terrorism, and unrest. However, millions visit safely each year by staying vigilant, avoiding high-risk areas, and using reliable transportation. The city's central neighborhoods like Plateau and Zone 4 are generally safer than outlying areas.

neighborhoods.name: Plateau
neighborhoods.description: The business district with hotels, embassies, and restaurants. Well-patrolled during the day with good infrastructure, though quieter at night.

neighborhoods.name: Zone 4
neighborhoods.description: Popular nightlife and entertainment area with restaurants, bars, and clubs. Generally safe but requires caution during late hours.

neighborhoods.name: Cocody
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential area with shopping centers and the university. Relatively safe but visitors should remain aware of surroundings.

neighborhoods.name: Marcory
neighborhoods.description: Mixed commercial and residential area with some nightlife venues. Exercise increased caution, especially after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Treichville
neighborhoods.description: Dense urban area with markets and local businesses. Higher crime rates reported, particularly for petty theft and pickpocketing.

neighborhoods.name: Yopougon
neighborhoods.description: Large residential commune with limited tourist infrastructure. Avoid unnecessary visits, especially at night due to elevated crime risks.

scams.name: Fake Official Scam
scams.description: Criminals pose as police officers or government officials demanding bribes or document fees from tourists.
scams.howToAvoid: Always ask for proper identification, insist on going to an official police station, and never pay money on the spot.

scams.name: Overcharging Taxi Scam
scams.description: Taxi drivers quote inflated fares to tourists, especially from the airport or to popular destinations.
scams.howToAvoid: Use metered orange taxis, agree on fare before departure, or use ride-hailing apps like Yango for transparent pricing.

scams.name: ATM and Money Exchange Fraud
scams.description: Criminals target tourists at ATMs or offer unofficial currency exchange at inflated rates with counterfeit bills.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks or hotels, avoid street money changers, and exchange money only at official locations.

scams.name: Distraction Theft
scams.description: Groups work together to distract tourists while accomplices steal bags, phones, or valuables in markets and busy areas.
scams.howToAvoid: Keep valuables secure, be wary of overly friendly strangers, and avoid displaying expensive items like jewelry or electronics.

soloFemale.overview: Solo female travelers can visit Abidjan but need to take extra precautions. Sexual harassment and unwanted attention are concerns, particularly in crowded areas and at night. The local culture is generally respectful but conservative dress is recommended. Many women travel solo successfully by staying in safer neighborhoods, using reliable transportation, and connecting with other travelers or expatriate communities.
tips: Dress conservatively covering shoulders and knees, especially when visiting religious sites or local neighborhoods
Avoid walking alone at night and use trusted transportation like hotel-recommended taxis or ride-hailing apps
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in safer areas like Plateau or Cocody rather than budget options in outlying areas
Trust your instincts if a situation feels uncomfortable and don't hesitate to seek help from hotel staff or other travelers
Keep emergency contacts readily available and inform someone of your daily plans when exploring the city

nightSafety.overview: Abidjan's nightlife can be enjoyable but requires careful planning and awareness. Crime rates increase after dark, and the US embassy restricts its employees from driving outside major cities at night. Popular nightlife areas like Zone 4 and Plateau are relatively safer but still require vigilance. Avoid outlying neighborhoods after dark and always use reliable transportation.
tips: Stick to well-known nightlife areas like Zone 4, Plateau, and parts of Cocody with good lighting and security presence
Always use trusted transportation - never walk between venues at night, even short distances
Go out in groups when possible and keep track of your companions throughout the evening
Avoid displaying expensive items like jewelry, watches, or large amounts of cash when out at night
Plan your return transportation in advance and avoid waiting alone on streets for taxis or rides

transport.metro: Abidjan lacks a complete metro system. Public transport relies mainly on shared taxis (woro-woros) and minibuses (gbakas) which can be confusing for tourists and are not recommended for first-time visitors due to unmarked routes and informal stops.
transport.rideshare: Uber recently exited the market in 2025, but Yango (Russian-owned) is the dominant ride-hailing app, offering upfront pricing and GPS tracking. Other options include inDrive and Heetch. These apps are generally safer and more reliable than street taxis for tourists.
transport.taxis: Orange metered taxis are the official option and generally safer for tourists, though meters are rarely used so negotiate fares upfront. Shared taxis (woro-woros) are color-coded by route but difficult for visitors to navigate. Airport taxis charge premium rates.
transport.tips: Download Yango app before arrival for reliable rides. For airport transfers, use official taxis or pre-book through your hotel. Avoid motorcycle taxis and unmarked vehicles. Always negotiate taxi fares before departure or insist on the meter.

customs: Greet people with a handshake; close friends may exchange cheek kisses
Dress modestly, especially in religious areas - cover shoulders and knees
Remove shoes when entering someone's home
Use your right hand for eating, greeting, and giving/receiving items
Show respect for elders and traditional authorities
Avoid public displays of affection
Ask permission before photographing people, especially in traditional settings

health.overview: Health risks in Abidjan include malaria, yellow fever, and waterborne diseases. The city has decent medical facilities compared to rural areas, but standards may not meet those of developed countries. Private clinics generally offer better care than public hospitals.
health.water: Tap water is technically potable but bottled water is strongly recommended for tourists. Ice may be made from tap water, so request drinks without ice or ensure it's from bottled water.
health.vaccinations: Yellow fever vaccination is mandatory for entry and must be administered at least 10 days before arrival. Malaria prophylaxis is essential as the risk is high year-round. Consider hepatitis A/B, typhoid, and meningitis vaccines.
health.altitude: Abidjan is at sea level with a hot, humid tropical climate. The rainy season (May-November) brings heavy rains and flooding risks. Air conditioning and mosquito protection are essential year-round.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Abidjan: +225-27-22-49-40-00 (24hr emergency: +225-27-22-49-44-50). Located at Cocody Riviera Golf, 01 BP 1712 Abidjan 01

faq.q: Is Abidjan safe for tourists?
faq.a: Abidjan can be safe for tourists who exercise appropriate caution. The US State Department has a Level 2 advisory citing crime and security concerns, but many visitors have safe trips by staying alert, avoiding high-risk areas, and using reliable transportation.

faq.q: Is Abidjan safe at night?
faq.a: Nighttime safety requires extra caution in Abidjan. Stick to well-lit, popular areas like Zone 4 and Plateau, always use trusted transportation, and avoid walking alone. Crime rates increase after dark, so plan your evening activities carefully.

faq.q: Is Abidjan safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Solo female travelers can visit Abidjan but need additional precautions. Dress conservatively, stay in safer neighborhoods, use reliable transportation, and be prepared for potential harassment. Many women travel solo successfully with proper planning.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Abidjan?
faq.a: Avoid Treichville and Yopougon, especially at night, due to higher crime rates. Also avoid the northern border regions of Ivory Coast entirely due to terrorism risks. Stick to central areas like Plateau, Zone 4, and Cocody.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Abidjan?
faq.a: Public minibuses (gbakas) and shared taxis can be challenging for tourists due to unmarked routes and crowding. Orange metered taxis and ride-hailing apps like Yango are safer options. Avoid motorcycle taxis which are officially banned.

cities.name: New Orleans
cities.summary: New Orleans has experienced significant improvements in safety, with violent crime dropping 55% since 2022 and reaching historic lows. While petty crime and tourist scams remain concerns in busy areas, enhanced security measures and strong police presence in tourist districts have increased overall safety for visitors.
cities.quickVerdict: New Orleans is experiencing a safety renaissance, with murders at their lowest levels since the 1970s and continued crime reductions through 2025. The French Quarter and main tourist areas benefit from increased security, especially after the January 2025 incident led to enhanced safety protocols. While visitors should remain aware of pickpockets, scams, and avoid isolated areas at night, the city is significantly safer than its reputation suggests. The famous nightlife and cultural attractions can be enjoyed responsibly with basic precautions.

neighborhoods.name: French Quarter
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart of New Orleans with heavy police presence, enhanced security measures, and regular patrols. Tourist-friendly with well-lit streets, though crowded areas may attract pickpockets.

neighborhoods.name: Garden District
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential area with beautiful mansions and tree-lined streets. Generally very safe for tourists during day and evening, popular for walking tours and sightseeing.

neighborhoods.name: Central Business District
neighborhoods.description: Downtown business area near hotels and convention center. Safe during business hours but can empty out at night. Well-patrolled and good for tourists staying in area hotels.

neighborhoods.name: Marigny/Frenchmen Street
neighborhoods.description: Trendy nightlife area with live music venues. Generally safe for tourists but use caution late at night. Stick to well-lit main streets and avoid empty side streets.

neighborhoods.name: Central City
neighborhoods.description: Residential area with higher crime rates. Not recommended for tourists, especially at night. Limited attractions and poor lighting make this area best avoided.

neighborhoods.name: Desire/Upper Ninth Ward
neighborhoods.description: Area still recovering from Hurricane Katrina with high crime rates. Tourists should avoid this neighborhood entirely, particularly after dark. No major tourist attractions to warrant visits.

scams.name: Shoe Scam
scams.description: Someone approaches complimenting your shoes, bets they can tell you where you got them, then demands payment after saying 'on your feet in New Orleans.'
scams.howToAvoid: Don't engage with strangers who comment on your appearance. Keep walking and avoid eye contact with persistent street hustlers.

scams.name: Fake Street Performers
scams.description: People offer to take your photo then demand payment, or approach with 'free' drinks that come with surprise charges up to $20.
scams.howToAvoid: Only ask clearly identified business staff to take photos. Never accept items from strangers on the street, even if they claim it's free.

scams.name: Parking Lot Scam
scams.description: Unofficial 'attendants' collect parking fees in lots they don't own, then disappear, leaving your car vulnerable to tickets or towing.
scams.howToAvoid: Only park in official lots with clear signage and receipts. Be suspicious of handwritten signs and cash-only operations with lawn chairs.

scams.name: Fortune Teller Hustle
scams.description: Street fortune tellers offer readings then demand high payments or claim you need expensive spiritual cleansing to remove curses.
scams.howToAvoid: Visit established, licensed fortune tellers in proper businesses. Avoid anyone who approaches you on the street offering readings or spiritual services.

soloFemale.overview: New Orleans can be safely enjoyed by solo female travelers with proper precautions. Many women report positive experiences, especially during daytime hours and in well-populated tourist areas. The city has improved significantly in recent years, though standard urban safety practices remain essential.
tips: Stay in well-lit, populated areas, especially the French Quarter and Garden District
Use rideshare apps like Uber or Lyft instead of walking alone at night
Join group tours or bar crawls to meet other travelers and explore safely
Keep alcohol consumption moderate to stay alert and aware of surroundings
Book accommodation near main tourist areas to minimize late-night travel

nightSafety.overview: New Orleans nightlife is generally safe in tourist areas with enhanced security measures, but requires extra caution. Bourbon Street and the French Quarter have significant police presence, while Frenchmen Street offers a more authentic music scene. The January 2025 incident led to improved security protocols.
tips: Stay on main, well-lit streets like Bourbon Street and Frenchmen Street
Travel in groups when possible and avoid isolated side streets or alleys
Use rideshare services instead of walking long distances at night
Keep valuables secure and be aware of pickpockets in crowded areas
Don't accept drinks from strangers and keep an eye on your beverages

transport.metro: New Orleans does not have a metro system. The city relies on buses, streetcars, and rideshare for public transportation.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Lyft operate throughout the city and are considered safe, reliable options. Particularly recommended for late-night travel. Always verify driver identity and vehicle details before getting in.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are available but can be expensive. Airport rates are around $36 to downtown. Generally safe but rideshare apps offer more convenience and transparency.
transport.tips: The historic streetcar system is charming and safe for daytime travel. RTA buses are reliable for getting around the city. For airport transfers, official shuttles cost $24 per person or use rideshare services.

customs: Embrace Southern hospitality - expect friendly greetings with 'ma'am' and 'sir' and engage in polite small talk
Learn po-boy etiquette - decide if you want it 'dressed' (with lettuce, tomato, mayo) and be ready for friendly food debates
Respect the 'laissez les bons temps rouler' culture - participate in local traditions and don't rush the relaxed pace of life

health.overview: New Orleans has good healthcare facilities including several major hospitals. The subtropical climate can be challenging with high humidity and heat. Water quality is generally good but can be affected by flooding.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink under normal conditions. During flooding events, follow local advisories. The 2021 Water Quality Report showed compliance with safety standards.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required. Standard US healthcare recommendations apply. Consider flu shots during winter months.
health.altitude: Sea level location with subtropical climate. Summers are very hot and humid (80-95°F), winters mild (50-70°F). Hurricane season runs June-November requiring weather monitoring.

emergency.usEmbassy: N/A - Domestic US destination. For international visitors, contact your home country's consulate. Major consulates located in nearby Houston, Texas.

faq.q: Is New Orleans safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, New Orleans is significantly safer than its reputation suggests. Violent crime has dropped 55% since 2022, reaching historic lows. Tourist areas like the French Quarter have enhanced security and regular police patrols, making them generally safe for visitors who take basic precautions.

faq.q: Is New Orleans safe at night?
faq.a: New Orleans nightlife areas are generally safe with proper precautions. Bourbon Street and the French Quarter have heavy security presence, especially after enhanced measures implemented in 2025. Stay on well-lit main streets, travel in groups when possible, and use rideshare services instead of walking alone.

faq.q: Is New Orleans safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Many solo female travelers visit New Orleans safely. Stay in tourist areas, use rideshare apps at night, join group tours, and keep alcohol consumption moderate. The French Quarter and Garden District are particularly safe during the day and evening hours.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in New Orleans?
faq.a: Avoid Central City, Desire District, and the Upper Ninth Ward, especially at night. These areas have higher crime rates and few tourist attractions. Even in safe areas, avoid empty side streets, alleys, and isolated areas after dark.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in New Orleans?
faq.a: Yes, public transport is generally safe. The historic streetcars are charming and safe for daytime travel. RTA buses are reliable city-wide. For late-night travel, Uber and Lyft are recommended as safer alternatives to walking or public transport.

cities.name: Washington DC
cities.summary: Washington DC has experienced dramatic improvements in safety throughout 2025, with violent crime dropping 28% year-over-year and homicides reaching historic lows. While property crime remains elevated and certain areas require caution, tourist zones like the National Mall and major museums are well-secured. The city combines federal-level security infrastructure with strong public transportation and improving neighborhood safety trends.
cities.quickVerdict: Washington DC is generally safe for tourists in 2026, particularly in major tourist areas which benefit from extensive federal security measures. The dramatic improvement in crime statistics throughout 2025 has created the strongest safety foundation the city has seen in years. While visitors should exercise standard urban caution regarding property crime and avoid certain neighborhoods at night, the overall safety trajectory is positive and tourist-focused areas remain highly secure.

neighborhoods.name: National Mall & Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Heavily patrolled federal area with multiple layers of security. Home to major monuments, museums, and government buildings with constant police presence and surveillance.

neighborhoods.name: Georgetown
neighborhoods.description: Historic upscale neighborhood with low crime rates, well-lit streets, and active foot traffic. Popular waterfront area with good restaurant and shopping safety.

neighborhoods.name: Dupont Circle
neighborhoods.description: Vibrant area with embassies, galleries, and nightlife. Well-maintained with good lighting and regular police patrols. Safe for solo travelers day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Capitol Hill
neighborhoods.description: Mixed safety profile with tourist areas around the Capitol being secure, but some blocks experiencing petty theft and burglaries. Enhanced security near government buildings.

neighborhoods.name: Shaw & U Street Corridor
neighborhoods.description: Gentrifying area with vibrant nightlife but ongoing safety concerns. Some blocks still struggle with theft, car break-ins, and assaults, especially around nightlife areas.

neighborhoods.name: Southeast DC (Anacostia)
neighborhoods.description: Higher crime rates including violent offenses. Limited tourist attractions and infrastructure. Visitors should exercise extreme caution and avoid after dark.

scams.name: Metro Change Scam
scams.description: Scammers approach tourists asking to exchange bills (like 2 tens for a twenty), then quickly switch out real bills for fake ones or short-change victims during the transaction.
scams.howToAvoid: Never exchange money with strangers. Use official currency exchange services or bank ATMs. If approached, politely decline and walk away immediately.

scams.name: Fake Booking Websites
scams.description: Fraudulent travel websites that mimic legitimate hotel or tour booking sites, stealing credit card information and personal data while providing no actual services.
scams.howToAvoid: Book directly through official hotel websites or verified platforms. Check URLs carefully for spelling errors. Verify bookings by calling hotels directly.

scams.name: Street Item Sales
scams.description: Individuals approaching tourists with items like jewelry, electronics, or artwork, often claiming special deals or emergency situations requiring quick cash.
scams.howToAvoid: Never purchase items from street sellers approaching you. Legitimate vendors operate from established locations with proper permits. Just say no and keep walking.

scams.name: Phone/Charity Solicitation
scams.description: Scammers asking to use your phone for emergency calls or requesting donations for fake charities, potentially installing malware or gathering personal information.
scams.howToAvoid: Never let strangers use your phone. Direct people needing help to nearby businesses or police officers. Research charities before donating.

soloFemale.overview: Washington DC is considered quite safe for solo female travelers, especially in tourist areas and well-populated neighborhoods. The significant crime reductions in 2025 have improved the overall environment for women traveling alone. Most female travelers report feeling comfortable during the day and in evening hours when staying in recommended neighborhoods with good lighting and foot traffic.
tips: Stick to well-lit, populated areas especially after dark - Georgetown, Dupont Circle, and the National Mall area are ideal
Use Metro or rideshare services rather than walking alone at night, particularly when traveling between neighborhoods
Stay alert in crowded tourist areas where pickpocketing can occur, and keep valuables secured in anti-theft bags

nightSafety.overview: Night safety in Washington DC varies significantly by location. Tourist areas like the National Mall remain relatively safe with illuminated monuments attracting visitors, while some neighborhoods require extra caution after dark. The improvement in violent crime rates throughout 2025 has made evening hours generally safer, but property crime remains a concern.
tips: Avoid isolated areas and stick to main streets with good lighting and regular foot traffic
Use rideshare or taxi services rather than walking alone, especially between different neighborhoods
Be aware that nightlife areas like Adams Morgan can get rowdy late at night, though they generally remain safe with proper precautions

transport.metro: The Washington Metro system has significantly improved safety in 2025, with crime on rail dropping 34% and new fare gates preventing evasion. The system is generally safe day and night, with increased police presence and better enforcement. Sit in the first car near the operator during late-night hours for added security.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Lyft operate extensively throughout DC with good safety records. Drivers must pass background checks and vehicles are tracked in real-time. Rideshare is often safer than walking alone at night and more reliable than traditional taxis, though surge pricing can apply during peak times.
transport.taxis: Traditional DC taxis are regulated and generally safe, though less convenient than rideshare apps. Official DC Taxi app available for smartphone booking. Ensure the taxi is licensed and use the meter. Taxis are readily available in tourist areas and at hotels.
transport.tips: The Metro is your best bet for safe, affordable transport. For late-night travel, rideshare services offer door-to-door convenience with tracking. Always verify your rideshare driver and vehicle details before getting in.

customs: Tipping is expected in restaurants (15-20%), for taxi drivers, hotel staff, and tour guides - it's considered part of service workers' income
Personal space norms include maintaining arm's length during conversations and firm handshakes for greetings in business contexts
Punctuality is highly valued - arrive on time for meetings, tours, and social gatherings as a sign of respect

health.overview: Washington DC has excellent healthcare infrastructure with multiple world-class hospitals including George Washington University Hospital, MedStar facilities, and Howard University Hospital. The city faces some health challenges including recent West Nile virus detections and higher STI rates, but medical care is readily accessible.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Washington DC. The city maintains high water quality standards and regular testing. Bottled water is widely available but unnecessary for health reasons.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for DC visits. Standard travel health precautions apply. Be aware of seasonal health advisories like West Nile virus during warmer months.
health.altitude: DC is at sea level with humid subtropical climate. Summers can be hot and humid, winters mild. Air conditioning is widely used. No altitude-related health concerns.

emergency.usEmbassy: N/A - Washington DC is the capital, but US citizens can contact State Department at 1-888-407-4747 from US/Canada or 1-202-501-4444 from abroad

faq.q: Is Washington DC safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Washington DC is generally safe for tourists, especially in major tourist areas like the National Mall, museums, and monuments which have extensive security. Crime has dropped significantly in 2025, with violent crime down 28% year-over-year.

faq.q: Is Washington DC safe at night?
faq.a: Night safety varies by area. Tourist zones and well-lit neighborhoods like Georgetown and Dupont Circle are generally safe. Avoid isolated areas and consider using rideshare or Metro rather than walking alone between neighborhoods after dark.

faq.q: Is Washington DC safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, DC is quite safe for solo female travelers. Stick to tourist areas and well-populated neighborhoods, use common sense precautions, and utilize safe transportation options like Metro or rideshare services, especially at night.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Washington DC?
faq.a: Avoid Southeast DC areas like Anacostia, especially at night. Some parts of Northeast DC also have higher crime rates. Tourist areas and Northwest neighborhoods like Georgetown are much safer.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Washington DC?
faq.a: Yes, the Metro system is generally safe and has improved significantly in 2025 with crime down 34% on rail and 37% on buses. New fare gates and increased police presence have enhanced security throughout the system.

cities.name: Boston
cities.summary: Boston is one of America's safest major cities with historically low crime rates, excellent healthcare, and strong infrastructure. While homicides increased modestly in 2025 to 31 cases, this remains well below the five-year average and maintains Boston's position as significantly safer than most metropolitan areas of similar size.
cities.quickVerdict: Boston consistently ranks among America's safest large cities, with violent crime rates 35% lower than Philadelphia and property crime rates nearly half that of similar urban centers. The city's 2025 statistics show a total crime rate of just 26.19 per 1,000 residents, meaning only about 2.6% of the population faces potential crime exposure annually. Public transportation reported a 13% reduction in major crimes, and the city's world-class healthcare system includes five hospitals in Newsweek's global top rankings. Tourist areas like Back Bay, Beacon Hill, and Downtown are particularly safe, though visitors should remain alert for pickpockets and retail theft in busy shopping areas.

neighborhoods.name: Back Bay & Beacon Hill
neighborhoods.description: Historic upscale neighborhoods with excellent foot traffic, active community policing, and violent crime rates 25-35% below city average. Home to luxury shopping, fine dining, and well-maintained Victorian architecture.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown & Financial District
neighborhoods.description: Business district with strong police presence, well-lit streets, and heavy daytime foot traffic. Crime rates below citywide average, though tourists should watch for pickpockets near Faneuil Hall and Quincy Market.

neighborhoods.name: North End & Charlestown
neighborhoods.description: Historic waterfront neighborhoods with moderate property crime mainly involving bike theft and car break-ins. Well-lit streets and neighborhood watches keep violent crime well under city median.

neighborhoods.name: South Boston (Southie)
neighborhoods.description: Gentrified waterfront area with violent crime 32% below city average. Some bar-related incidents around Andrew Square and Broadway after dark, but generally safe with active police patrols.

neighborhoods.name: East Boston
neighborhoods.description: Mixed safety profile with Logan Airport and growing immigrant communities. Seaport area is low-crime, while Maverick Square and Eagle Hill see higher incidents. Overall crime rate 45% below US average.

neighborhoods.name: Dorchester & Roxbury
neighborhoods.description: Higher crime neighborhoods with violent crime rates well above city mean. Visitors should stick to main streets, avoid walking alone at night, and use rideshare services for transportation.

scams.name: Pickpocketing & Distraction Scams
scams.description: Coordinated teams operate in tourist areas, with one person spilling something on you while accomplices steal bags or pickpocket wallets. Common near Faneuil Hall, Boston Common, and retail districts.
